REVIEW_TEXT: The first flight of my year went well with Delta Connection. All staff from the check-in counter to stewardesses in-flight were friendly, helpful and accommodating. This regional flight offered comfortable Comfort Plus seats and complimentary snacks like granola bars, cookies, and chips coupled with a selection of beverages. Despite being delayed, the plane made it on time and I successfully made my connecting flight. And the view of Seattle from above was spectacular! If I had to be critical, the SkyPriority experience could have been a bit better as there weren’t enough counters for the volume of flights at the time and the security line was too long.

I’m at Maui airport right now and my flight from delta got canceled last minute, last night “due to weather” but many airlines still departing. I tried to find a delta employee or customer service in the airport and for my surprise no one could be found. I called delta customer service and the only option was to flight 3 days later, no help finding us a hotel, no vouchers for food or transportation, nothing! I purchased last minute Southwest and leaving here today. When I called customer service and asked details about getting refunded their reply was very vague and they were not sure of anything! I only flight delta and sky miles member for years. Terrible customer service. Don’t ever flight to Hawaii with delta. I’m done being delta loyal member.

I booked a flight, rental car and hotel with Delta. Their hotel was the “airport” hotel but there was no information about the hotel on the app and when I finally found it, it was an hour away! Terrible. And it was a half hour wait to reach customer service

I’m at Maui airport right now and my flight from delta got canceled last minute, last night “due to weather” but many airlines still departing. I tried to find a delta employee or customer service in the airport and for my surprise no one could be found. I called delta customer service and the only option was to flight 3 days later, no help finding us a hotel, no vouchers for food or transportation, nothing! I purchased last minute Southwest and leaving here today. When I called customer service and asked details about getting refunded their reply was very vague and they were not sure of anything! I only flight delta and sky miles member for years. Terrible customer service. Don’t ever flight to Hawaii with delta. I’m done being delta loyal member.

I purchased an international itinerary directly through Delta Air Lines and paid to select seats on all flight segments, including an 8-hour long-haul flight operated by Delta’s partner airline, Korean Air. During booking, Delta’s system allowed seat selection on all three legs and confirmed those selections before payment. I would not have purchased this ticket without that confirmation. After payment, my email confirmation showed the seat assignment for the final leg as missing. Delta’s system again allowed me to return to the reservation and select seats for that segment. Despite this, Delta and Korean Air later directed me back and forth, ultimately stating that seat selection on the Korean Air flight was “never permitted,” even though Delta’s own system accepted payment and confirmed the seats. The result was a paid service that was confirmed and then revoked, with no resolution or accountability. This was not a minor inconvenience. Delta sold, processed payment for, and confirmed a seat selection that it later refused to honor, instead deflecting responsibility to its partner airline. Delta continues to promote seamless partner airline integration, yet provides no protection for customers when those systems fail. The burden was placed entirely on the customer to navigate conflicting airline policies after the purchase was complete. Delta should cearly disclose when seat selection on partner airlines is not guaranteed. Stop accepting payment for services that cannot be honored.  Take responsibility when its booking system misrepresents availability. Based on this experience, I cannot recommend Delta for international itineraries involving partner airlines.

I would like to give a special shout out and thank Michael of delta airlines counter staff at MCO airport for being so helpful and friendly. It means a lot to get assistance when traveling internationally and there are unfamiliar procedures and routes

I had a heartbreaking day on my departure day. So when I was checking in at Delta, I was crying…( actually more like bawling). Well I wasn't at first, but when Ms Carol asked me how was my day, tears just fell and couldn’t stop it…felt heavy…so I really appreciate that question a lot, she gave me some small talk, which made me open up a bit and that’s when I started crying. She didn’t have to continue comforting me, but she did and I felt it in my heart. Ms Ammi saw me crying and offered me some juice and orange, and a Chocolate cupcake and also comforted me despite how busy they were. They are the best strangers I’ve encountered at that moment too, for the longest time, and that made me feel way lighter. So I think it’s not about the place, or the airline, it’s the people that works within - a truly unforgettable experience even though that was a month ago. I am talking about the Ground staff of Delta Airlines in Baltimore Airport! They did their jobs with passion and understanding, and I salute them all…

It was an easy online check in. Due to other company flight delays I was short on time to drop my bag in. When I arrived it was too late for me to print my bag tag. Ricky at LAX directed me to an operator who printed my tag and checked my bag in. Flight was also great. Service when needed but nice and quiet during the night to sleep. I recommend Delta Air Lines.

Worst airline experience ever. Had an international flight with a layover at JFK. While on the international flight, received notification that the connecting flight was canceled and rebooked for the following morning (16 hours later). After 2 phone calls and 1 visit to counter, received a hotel and a ride to and from hotel but still had to wait until next morning for flight. Arrived at airport at 3:45am for flight. Checked bags, had a boarding pass with assigned seat for the 6:25am flight out. At 5:12am, received an email stating that the confirmation was canceled and that Delta did not have us on a flight and to contact service for rebooking. So basically they removed us from the flight after assigning a seat. Another trip to the counter to be told they can put us on standby for a 9:50am flight. Then have to go to customer service to locate baggage once we arrive at final destination. So it’s a prayer that our luggage even arrives at our final destination at this point. Sit and wait an additional 3 hours at the airport for a standby flight now. We were Delta Comfort+ tickets but now standby and will be sitting in main cabin without any compensation. Will NOT be flying with Delta again. Their agents had no solutions and no compensation provided for any of the changes, inconveniences, time lost, downgrades of seats, etc. Delta is not as good as Spirit or JetBlue at this point and don’t give a damn about their customers.

Delta requires a mileage program to use wifi. Delta's account recovery gives me a mileage number for my identity, but then the login page doesn't exist. Trying to register a new account directly me back to the old number. To get out of this circle, I tried to contact support. There is only messaging support. To get help with a login problem, messaging support makes you log in. Which of course I can't. Delta has made their system perfectly incapable of recovering from a problem, and tied key features like wifi to that system. Not a good experience for a traveller who needs to do work.

They forced me to check a bag both ways due to size regiments of my carry on. Both trips they lost my checked bag. They offered no compensation for losing my bag during connecting flights. I was stranded in a foreign country with nothing. I submitted multiple claims for compensation and they were all immediately declined due to my ticket not being eligible for compensation. This was my first and last experience with Delta airlines.

When initially purchasing a Delta seat, upgrading to First Class is usually quite expensive. Therefore it is rare that they sell out their first class seats initially. When Delta doesn’t sell all those first class seats early on (typical), Delta will do a “reverse auction” by continually lowering the price of those first class seats, until all the seats are sold, usually at rock bottom prices. But Delta doesn’t want their best customers / Diamond Medallion to see how cheaply they are selling those seats to random customers, so they block us (Diamond Medallion / Loyal customers) from being able to upgrade to First for a fee. On the app, all those first class seats are just X’ed out and unavailable to us! So next time all you diamond medallion customers are sitting in comfort plus, and wondering who paid those huge prices to get into first class, please know that those are mostly non-loyal customers that bought the seat very inexpensively. And those inexpensive seats were never made available to you, Delta‘s best customers! Delta does not take care of the best customers.  I’ve already started flying other airlines.

At the gate broken navigation separation system 2 hours system not fixed. Then workaround and take on more fuel. Taxi for take off and air system broken 1 hour later back to the gate. I took an additional hour to cancel the flight. Very long line at customer service to get a $48 Food voucher. Not able to use at the second rate hotel we were sent to. Lack of information, bad communication. We had the Delta app that even after the flight was canceled sent notes the flight would be departing in 30 min. Sent us 16000 air miles, insulting. We had upgraded seats that we never received.

Delay after delay after gate change after delay. I'm looking at my plane right now, and 2 hours later we are starting to board. This happened on my previous flight too If you can't keep your schedule then close down/sell to someone who can. My time is worth a decent amount, and you have taken over 7 hours total of delays (delays on the way there, and on the way back) that I could have been working and making money. At least they have video screens so it keeps my son occupied

My wife and I were placed in 2 separate rows. When we approached the ticket agent to check in our luggage, we asked if my wife could be moved to the same row as me. The agent started yapping on about how we should get an American express card while checking in our luggage, going on & on about all the benefits. Finally my wife's aisle was moved to mine. We boarded the plane and flew to Orlando for our vacation. After our vacation I checked the charges and found Delta charged me twice for seat changes. Apparently, the ticketing agent moved me over a single seat in the same row from seat B to C (I did not ask for that). I jumped through all their customer service hoops to get refunded for 1 of the seat moves only to be told that since we flew, no refund was required.
This has to be the worst airline flying. We have been sitting at the gate in Atlanta for 7 hours and they just brought the plane from the hanger. Were they unaware of the flight???

This is a mediocre airline masking chaos with spin. My recent flight experience with Delta was an absolute disaster. The flight was scheduled to leave at 5 PM, and we had already begun pre-boarding when they suddenly delayed it—due to “crew rotation issues solvable in 30 min”. That’s not just bad planning, that’s complete operational failure. Worse yet, Delta kept passengers in limbo by delaying the flight in 3–4 hour increments, rather than being honest about the scope of the issue. We didn’t end up departing until 3 AM—nearly 10 hours late. This wasn’t just an inconvenience; I missed an important business meeting in Seattle that cannot be rescheduled or salvaged. When I asked about meal vouchers, a check-in agent smugly claimed the delay was due to weather—a completely false excuse that he threw out without even checking the flight’s status. When I pressed him on it, he got defensive instead of just owning the mistake. Another agent eventually handed me a $45 voucher, which was frankly insulting considering the time and opportunities lost. Meanwhile, the ground crew was patronizing and dismissive, treating passengers like they were the problem for simply asking for updates. The lack of accountability was staggering. But here’s the real kicker: This isn’t just a one-off incident. Delta has clearly been in a state of decline. Flights are constantly delayed, crews are visibly irritated, customer service is unresponsive, and compensation is laughably inadequate. It’s also hard to ignore the pattern: Every quarter, especially in the last two weeks before it ends, Delta’s delays and cancellations seem to spike—and yet, minimal compensation is offered to retail passengers. It’s almost as if they’re gaming the system: delaying flights incrementally to avoid triggering certain metrics, and downplaying the chaos to maintain the illusion of operational performance. It feels calculated—like Delta is more concerned with managing optics and investor reports than actually serving its customers. This is not an isolated issue. It’s systemic. They lost me as a business.

I arrived at the airport and my flight was delayed. My Delta app notified me I could switch flights for free, so I did switch so that I could catch my second flight from Seattle to Redmond (My first flight was Boston to Seattle). This second flight was delayed, and my app notified me I could switch flights for free, and so I switched to my original flight from Boston to Seattle with a later flight from Seattle to Redmond. When my flight was finally ready for boarding (4 hours after expected time), I tried to scan my physical boarding pass and was told I could not board the flight. I figured this was just because I switched flights in the app, so I showed them my boarding pass in the app. They said I did not have an eticket (despite my app showing my itinerary) and that I would not be allowed to board. I asked to speak to a manager, and she told me since I didn't have an eticket I would not be on the flight, nor could I transfer to another flight or receive a refund. I showed them my original boarding pass, my flight receipt, my app boarding pass, my app itinerary, and my baggage status (showing my bags were on that flight) in the app. They let me watch my flight take off without me. I was able to book a flight with United later in the day and that flight went smoothly. I still have yet to see if my checked baggage makes it to Redmond, the Delta app says it's on the plane but who knows. 0/10 They didn't let me on my flight that I paid $350 for and they might lose my baggage.

The Feb 21st Delta flight from Detroit to Amsterdam has been delayed for over 3 hours due to a technical issue (allegedly, a faulty switch on the board). The communication on the steps taken to remediate has been proactive and constant, while the Delta app has been far behind in terms of updates during the whole time. However, the communication on the available options couldn't have been worse. One cabin crew said we could offboard and come back, another said the opposite. When redirected to a manager, I have been told that we would deplane all deplane shortly. Less than two minutes later, another cabin crew announced that we'd be done in 15 minutes. And so on and so forth for 3.5 hours now. One cabin crew in particular has been quite rude when asked for clarifications. He patronizingly retorted that maybe I shouldn't get off the plane if I'm concerned about being able to get back on. How would anyone not be concerned given the circumstances?! During all these hours, the only thing we've been offered was water. No food, no snacks, no any other beverages! And not for lack of asking.

First the good. An incredible lead flight attendant who completely won us all over with his charm, his smile, and his efficiency. He did not disappear after the meal service but consistently checked up on us offering snacks and drinks.The meal was a pre order and ok. The insult is the seating configuration. The meager legroom should be an embarrassment to Delta. It is literally impossible for the window seater to get to the aisle without the aisle sitter getting up.When the seat in front is reclined minimally there is not an inch to spare and the screen is hard to see. Directly behind without a divider of any sort is a small premium economy section who sit there enviously staring at the minimal upgrade that first class is. Truly shameful. As I said previously the flight attendant was the saving grace.

I could not purchase check-in luggage until online check-in opened 24 hours prior to the flight. This is inconvenient as I was travelling and busy in the 24 hours leading up to the flight. Why can’t they allow purchase of check-in luggage at the time of booking, saving passengers a last minute scramble to sort things out? Makes no sense to me. Baggage drop at Orlando was smooth (no queue) but the flight ended up being 5 hours delayed. Whilst the weather is out of their hands, the airline’s communication and contingency planning was lacking. Delta staff at the airport couldn’t care less about delayed passengers, and I found out more of what was going on via my own internet searches. Onboard service was pretty basic (a drink and a single cookie on a 3 hour flight) and inconsistent (some passengers in the same class were offered more than others).

Delta has no control over weather, they certainly have control over what planes land. There were storms in Atlanta causing delays on April 6. Our flight circled Atlanta for an hour and a half, and then was low on gas. Rather than land us, they diverted us to Greensboro South Carolina, where we sat for over 3 1/2 hours. As a direct result of this, I miss my flight to Italy, a gentleman and a seat across from me missed his flight to Finland, and they were multiple other passengers who also missed flights, both domestically and internationally from this one flight alone. At Atlanta, there were two maybe three agents to help every single customer who are disrupted by the weather. The online system put you on hold for an hour, then get back to you an hour and say oh, we’re gonna put you on hold for another hour before somebody answers. There is literally no excuse for an airline of Delta size to have insufficient customer service representatives when they know they are subject to weather in Atlanta. This is not the first time Delta’s been through this.

For the past five years, every single flight I’ve had with Delta has come with a guaranteed problem, whether it’s a multi-hour delay, a last-minute cancellation, or total silence from customer service when things go wrong. There is no transparency, no accountability, and absolutely no sense of urgency or empathy for travelers who are stranded, late for work, missing connections, or simply trying to get home. Delta does not care. They don’t care about compensating passengers. They don’t care about clear communication. And they definitely don’t care about the inconvenience they cause. This airline has become synonymous with stress and disappointment. Fly with Delta only if you enjoy chaos, being treated like an afterthought, and wasting entire days of your life.

Delta canceled Flight DL 930 due to a mechanical issue. Delta was unable to rebook us on any alternative flights. As a result, my family was forced to remain at the airport overnight. No hotel or transportation assistance was provided. Eventually, we had no option but to return home by Uber at our own expense. Delta has since refused to reimburse our transportation costs, even though the cancellation was due to a mechanical failure, which is fully within Delta’s control. Additionally, the airport staff were unprofessional and unable to provide clear assistance. For every basic question they had to make phone calls, acting only as intermediaries without authority to resolve the situation. After many hours, we were simply told that there were “no flights” available. The cancellation process itself took approximately five hours to complete, creating unnecessary stress and hardship, especially while traveling with a child. Delta’s handling of this situation reflects a serious failure in customer care, communication, and basic passenger protection obligations.

We didn’t get to fly with Delta as they canceled our flight 2 hours before we were leaving home. The rescheduled us, but on a flight that wouldn’t get in until 5 minutes before our cruise left the harbor - we were not going to make it! We called around frantically & booked an expensive flight on United - never got reimbursed for that by our travel insurance (don’t bother!). We were disappointed that they didn’t care if we missed our cruise, didn’t reimburse or refund us. Never again!

I checked in 2 luggages, in my first layover I received a message that one of my luggages end up in Tarom lost and found office in Bucharest,  a totally different airline.Because my full journey included 2 layovers I been told to fill a complaint when reach final destination. Nevertheless my luggage was returned to my home address, which cause a big inconvenience for me as I work on ship and this luggage was essential to me. As per company policy, you are entitle for reimbursement for delay luggage into 21 days frame. Was not the case, as although my claim was made in time, their answer came after 30 days with conclusion I exceed the time frame. This led me to believe was intentionally done and their negligence from check in to handle this situation is serious misconduct.

This is the second time we have flown Delta in 6 months. Both times we ended up getting home a day later. I usually look for the cheapest flight when I travel. I will continue to do that but I will never travel Delta again. I would rather pay more for any other airline. The last time they gave us a token amount that wasn’t worth the head ache.
My flight was on August 26  at 5:20 AM. Flight Number DL812. This flight had the most professional and courteous flight crew I have ever encountered. Exceptional customer service!

Flew back home to New York on New Yewrs Eve. Check in in Los Angeles was easy and efficient. Extremely friendly agents at the gate area and very professional crew on the flight. I chose to sit on the back of the plane since there were many empty seats and thankfully nobody sat next to me which made the flight more comfortable. For this 4.5 hour flight the crew was very professional and attentive also very friendly. The aircraft was one of the refurbished 767s and even though it was old the cabin was in great shape with excellent WiFi and delta studio which is amazing. The crew came by with three drink services they also passed around with water. Compared to my last flight on delta this one was outstanding. While exiting the plane I saw someone left an amenity kit and I asked the cabin attendant if I could have it she said sure. Recommend delta when they have a New York City based crew.

I arrive to TPA International to board my flight from Tampa to Las Vegas and the TSA airport security says that my date of birth with my booking reservation with Delta is wrong and it is 05/01/1997, but I talk to 4 delta representatives and they say that my birthdate with my booking reservation was correct and has not changed since I originally booked my flight. I miss my flight and I am forced to pay $600 for a new plane ticket because TSA makes me go through ID Verification and then I try to get a refund for my flight ticket and Delta says that their system is correct and my birthday was right the entire time. Both Delta and Tampa International both are just pointing fingers at each other and I am the one who has to front the $600 expense and both companies say it’s not their fault. Terrible customer service from each side and no one wants to take accountability and just default to “it’s our procedure” or the booking reservation has not changed from the beginning. Also the TSA Supervisor is condescending and tried to make it my fault and can’t even tell me why it’s coming up as incorrect if delta says it’s correct the entire time.

Delta ought to be ashamed flying this ancient aircraft on this route. Middle biz class seats have no divider insuring zero privacy. The screen size is the same as my IPad, 3 out of 6 toilets inoperable. While lie flat seats are very narrow. Calling this Delta One is inexcusable. Food was mediocre. Crew worked hard and pleasantly but could do just so much.I felt sorry for them. Wifi of course did not work.

Haven't flown Delta in a while, I see why. I am only giving this review 2 because of the Boston crew flying out of Dallas acknowledged my birthday and gave me a treat. But everything else Delta went downhill. In Atlanta our flight was delayed, no explanation. Being proactive, I told the gate person that I was in zone 8 and couldn't check my bag because I would be late for my cruise check in. He didn't care, checked my bag anyway. When we arrived in Palm Beach, I was too late to board the cruise, so I missed the cruise by 10 minutes. Then having to get hotel and car to get around Palm Beach because there were no affordable flights back home. Get to the airport to return home, again flight delayed, missed connecting flight. 7pm home arrival turned into 1am. This was the worst birthday trip ever. Date of experience: June 18, 2025

I bought two tickets for my friends. The payment went through, and I received a confirmation of the purchase of two tickets. Everything is fine. But imagine the surprise when we were told at the airport that the tickets were cancelled, and Delta sent me a refund. I did not request a refund, my bank (as I found out) did not reject the transaction. For some reason unknown to me, Delta decided to do this. And Delta did not notify anyone. And we get a situation - two people at the airport cannot fly, because they just found out that Delta cancelled their tickets.

I am writing to formally express my dissatisfaction with the service we experienced during our recent travel attempt with Delta Airlines, which not only resulted in three flight cancellations in one day but also caused us to miss a significant family event. We had booked a trip to Atlanta well in advance to attend a wedding on Saturday, August 2nd. Intentionally, we selected a 10:00 AM flight from Cleveland to allow ample buffer time in case of any unforeseen delays. Unfortunately, this flight was cancelled without explanation. I immediately attempted to rebook and was placed on the next available flight at 6:00 PM, as all earlier flights were fully booked. The 6:00 PM flight was also cancelled later that afternoon. Upon contacting Delta, I was informed that the only viable option to reach Atlanta in time for the wedding was to drive two hours to Columbus for a 6:30 PM departure. We were advised to save our gas and parking receipts for reimbursement. We made that drive and arrived at 5:30 PM. While checking in, I inquired about an earlier 5:30 PM Columbus-Atlanta flight (which was delayed at the time) but was told it was full and had at least a 20-person standby list. Shortly thereafter, I was told my 6:30 PM flight was delayed to 9pm, then was cancelled—our third cancellation of the day. From 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM, our day was consumed by repeated cancellations, rebookings, and a 4-hour round trip drive, all in an unsuccessful effort to reach Atlanta for our wedding. Meanwhile, other Delta flights from both Cleveland and Columbus to Atlanta departed without issue, and family members and friends on those flights arrived without incident. If weather was cited as the cause for our cancellations, it remains unclear why numerous other flights on the same route, at overlapping times, operated as scheduled. The cancellations appear arbitrary, and I suspect they were due to staffing iand plane availability issues, not just weather. I was later informed 2 of the cancelleations were due to inadequate staffing for the flight. To add to our frustration, our submission for reimbursement of gas and parking expenses related to our drive to Columbus—initiated under the guidance of Delta staff—was denied. Given the extent of inconvenience we endured, and the fact that we ultimately missed an important family event, I had reasonably expected some form of compensation. A denial of basic out-of-pocket expenses was not anticipated. My wife spent hours the following day working to cancel and request refunds for our unused hotel accommodations in Atlanta—another unfortunate outcome of the travel disruptions. This experience has left us extremely disappointed in the level of service and customer care provided by Delta. At the very least, we expected that our incurred travel expenses would be reimbursed. Beyond that, we believe additional compensation is warranted given the significant disruption to our travel plans caused by Delta's inadequate staffing issues, the missed wedding, and the lack of meaningful support or resolution offered throughout this ordeal. I can no longer access my boarding pass for verification, it's not available on the App.

The Delta negotiations and lack of communication misconnected my flight to Europe by 30 hr and refused reimburse me my out of pocket hotel and cab expenses. To top it off they lost my luggage for 3 days even after I asked Delta to make sure to put on the next flight. I hope people start to boycott Delta that they will lose a lot of money maybe then will start respecting people who made them successful.

As an experienced traveller I can tell that traveling with Delta is the worst experience. It starts with the ground staff who are very unfriendly, impatient and it clearly shows that they don’t enjoy their job. Following the airline crew, members feels like all mid aged to old women who passed their prime. Hence service is taken superficially and impatiently. No sense of customer service and friendliness. I will avoid Delta from now on.

SEA to MFR in first class. Flight slightly delayed but the pilot made up for it in the air. Ground staff kept everyone informed. Even on this routine domestic flight, the crew reflected how diverse the U.S. aviation workforce is - people from different backgrounds and life paths, all clearly well-trained, professional people who enjoy their jobs. All in all a good experience.

New design for the overhead compartment. Call lights and light also new. FA were very particular with backpacks placed under the seat. Only few seats for comfort plus. Boarding was confusing initially i was in main cabin 17C the upgraded to 13A and upon boarding back to my original seat. Super delayed flight due to staffing and scheduling as was announced. Card for platinum holders was given a note on the seat for the 100 years anniversary

This was the first time we flew with delta, our 1st flight to our destination went very smooth. Our flight on our way home that's something else, flew in to Atlanta set on the tarmac for 3 hour while it was raining, after we got to the gate they canceled our connecting flight. Went to customer service to some lady that didn't care. Was told that the next flight wasn't until Sunday at noon mind, you this is Friday night at 1030 pm. it's take it or leave it, Making it impossible to get your money back or get hotels. This will be the only time I ever fly this airline.

I recently travelled with Delta Air Lines, and my experience was a stark contrast between professionalism and utter incompetence. First, I must commend the telephone customer support team in Japan. When a technical system error occurred, they handled the situation with remarkable sincerity and professional integrity. Their dedication provided me with great peace of mind. However, the experience at the Honolulu airport counter was deeply disappointing. A staff member displayed a shockingly dismissive and overbearing attitude, frequently cutting me off, showing a lack of patience. Fortunately, another staff member eventually intervened with a professional and empathetic approach to resolve the issue.

Flew first class, 6 hour flight cold cabin and no blankets available in first class. Unbelievable on cross country flight in first class, no blanket, no pillow, no amenities. Worst first class ever, don’t waste your money

Dallas/Fort Worth to Seattle on the A220-100. I went to the airport 3.5 hours before departure, and at that time, the check-in counters were not open yet. The kiosks were, and I was able to get my boarding pass and bag tag before queuing. The number of early morning flights departing out of Dallas meant that the line was long, but Delta did a great job managing crowd control. The ground staff members at Dallas were helpful and communicative, but not the friendliest, as if they were not having a great night's sleep and hated working opening shifts (which is understandable). I then proceeded to my gate and boarded at the very last group, welcomed by a team of flight crews who were more passionate. Take-off was only a few negligible minutes behind. My seat, being a row behind the Comfort+ row and ahead of the emergency exit, did not recline, but it came with 1" extra pitch at 32" and was decently comfortable, clean, and user-friendly. Delta provides both IFE TV screens that were locally developed by DFP as well as Wi-Fi connectivity. Both were super responsive, and their interfaces were laid out beautifully. The contents on the TV were loaded and plenty, with the only issue being the flight map not advancing past the information screen. The Wi-Fi was surprisingly also very stable, even when playing 1080p YouTube videos. The meal, despite only being snacks, was decent - the crews offered 3 rounds of drink services on this 4-hour flight. My aircraft was around 6.5 years old at the time of my ride, and the cabin condition made it seem like the aircraft was only half its actual age. The air conditioning was set at a great temperature, and I didn't see any cleanliness issues on board. Having flown other US carriers, I must say that Delta's cabin crews have proven on my flight that they are a league above anyone else; they were super friendly and energetic, treating everybody with a smile, respect, and attentiveness. Landing was a few minutes early, but disembarkation was delayed due to the gate staff not being ready, which added to the lateness. Upon arrival, my bags were already waiting for me. Overall, Delta barely put a foot wrong on this flight, and I feel appreciated as a Skymiles member.

When we arrived at the airport, we headed to the kiosk to get our baggage tags. Us being senior citizens are always a little confused how to work the machines. A Delta Employee named Mitra, quickly came up to us and helped us all the way thru the system. She was so pleasant and helpful! A shout out to Mitra! Sometimes there is nobody around to get help.

My flight from Asheville, NC, to Atlanta was delayed due to an aircraft issue, causing a very late arrival at 1:30 AM and a 6:00 AM connecting flight. Delta provided a hotel voucher and a $12 food voucher (usable only at the hotel), which offered minimal value at that hour. Upon returning to the airport, I requested a Comfort or Comfort+ upgrade from the gate agent, hoping for a more comfortable experience after the overnight disruption. I was told all upgraded seats were full. Once onboard, I observed multiple unoccupied Comfort and Comfort+ seats. This lack of honesty was extremely disappointing. I understand fare class limitations, but being misled undermines trust. Transparency and integrity from gate staff are essential to maintaining Delta’s reputation for customer care.

Upon arrival for my flight from JFK-BUF on January 19 in terminal 4 the display board kept on showing “On time”, despite the fact the flight was canceled. Upon checking with the agent, I was offered another flight after a week, which was impossible for me to take. I couldn't believe they wanted me to stay in New York City for one week, even though I live 370 miles away in Rochester NY? Even when I checked the flight status after a day on Monday January 20, it was still showing “On Time”. How was this even possible? What an inefficiency. For this cancellation and misleading information of flight being “on time”, nothing was given to us as compensation or absolutely no assistance was provided to me. They simply lied and blamed everything on "weather" to shrug of their responsibility. The agent at the airport was rude and condescending. It has become a norm that all airlines blame weather to avoid paying any compensation.  I sent several emails via their online feedback form. No response. I called and waited for 3 hours. They told me to write the complaints via feedback form. So, I am going into an unending loop. All in wall I will never fly with Delta.

The passenger agents were fantastic, helpful and friendly at both, BDL and Atlanta, both to and from, The agent at ATL, Debbie was so helpful and friendly, she helped us, as I needed a wheelchair and she made sure it was all okay, and then she was the agent at the departure and just as efficient and pleasant under extreme pressure with a full plane and a medical emergency going on. The wheelchair agents that took me thru the airports to my flights were all so pleasant. All in All the trip was fantastic, the plane was comfortable, left on time and arrived on time. The TSA agents were very caring at both airports and helpful to me at my age and with all the metal in my body, thanks to them, what a difference when you have caring and down to earth people to help you. It was a pleasure to fly Delta.

Check in at JFK was okay the guy refused to allow me to check in at a counter and said I had to check in on a machine like everyone else so that was quite rude. Dropped the bag off and the lady was nice there, went through security very fast. The flight itself was okay. The offered a small snack They didn’t pick up the trash on the last five rows until 20 minutes before landing. Flight was on time. Inflight entertainment was awesome extensive amount of movies and shows, ample leg room so itself the flight was okay. You get free WiFi if you sign up for sky miles.

First time for me to travel on Delta in many years. I have to say I was very impressed. The plane was clean and new, the entertainment system worked smoothly, the free wifi was excellent. The service was sparse and with not too many smiles, but it was competent and professional. I flew from San Diego to JFK and back. And both flights were on time, and check in was smooth. Luggage was received quickly. Overall, quite pleased with the experience. I'd say they are a notch above Alaska and United from these two trips. Will look at flying them again if the destination and connection work out.

Flight from MIA to ATL Sunday 18 August: plane arrived late to the gate from ATL. Sitting by the window I saw the cleaning crew board and leave and then the gate agent announced a "maintenance issue." An hour later still not boarding. No communication on rebooking my connection (one hour layover in ATL so it would be missed). Delta chat agent (had to log on to that after already being logged into the app) claimed there were no later flights that day, so my checked luggage would be at the Baggage Service Office (BSO). I reserved a rental car to drive from ATL to MGM (my originating airport). After getting on the plane, pilot said the paperwork had to be re-done as it didn't meet the standards. Another hour delay with apathetic flight attendants. While on the plane Delta advised I was rebooked; there actually WAS another flight to MGM on Sunday. Arriving in ATL I went to the BSO and was told my luggage was going to MGM since it was rebooked. BUT that flight wasn't for another 3 hours and the rental car was non changable. So, train to the rental car center, 2 hours in the car to get home and drop off the fam. THEN an hour's drive to MGM to return the rental car and pick up the luggage, then home about 6-7 hours later than should have. AND the cat dogs had to be boarded an additional night vs. being picked up on Sunday. Delta is saying it's not reimbursing me the $279 for the rental car and I'm only entitled to a credit for future airfare.

Purchased 2 x 1st class tickets, direct from Delta, says lounge access in San Francisco and Atlanta. San Francisco no access, need to have a Delta credit or debit card, they sold these tickets on the promise of lounge access across all airports that you book with them. 13 years I've been with them, International and domestic. Never Again, treated like a second class human by staff at lounge. Since all tickets was purchased on credit card ,asking for a full refund.

Overbooked flight and allowed passengers carryon bags that boarded earlier to fill up all overhead bins such that the last third of the passengers had to check all carryon roller bags! Why can’t they only allow people to put their carryon bags overhead where they are sitting only? And overbooked the flight! Not a very good airline at all if they can’t control these simple things!

Reading about the supposed introduction of a "Basic" DL business class makes me think about my last flight on the airline. Boarding was by (a very slow) bus; the seats on the old A330 were uncomfortably narrow and coffin-like; lunch service was minimal and pretty gloppy. The supplied head phones weren't noise cancelling and so pretty useless (I got half the soundtrack on my own Boses --- they still have the ancient two-pin connection). Wine selection was pedestrian and so I wonder: how much more basic can business class really get? They did get me from there to here on time , but how much can you really subtract from this?

Took Delta for the first time for international travel to see my partner, who is deployed overseas. We're both military, so I'm used to bad flights but Delta really lowered the bar beyond my expectations. From PDX to ATH, had a transfer in CDG, first flight delayed, causing me to miss my transfer with a 10 hour layover. Large bag arrived damaged with tears. Heading back home to PDX, first flight was delayed almost making me miss my transfer. Arrive in SEA just to find out my bags are still in AMS and won't receive for a couple days since it's international. I packed for two weeks, so I have a decent amount of things including items required for work. Never had issues like this before with any airline until now. Terrible experience overall.

I decided to splurge and book a first class ticket on Delta last year for this year’s MegaCon Orlando. Outgoing was on a regional jet with hardly any of the amenities of the larger jets (I did get my drink served in a glass and several snacks) and having a dedicated Sky Priority lane at both RDU and MCO was helpful. Return flight was on their usual A319 jet with the full blown amenities. Both flights were on time and employees were fine.

My experience from Los Angeles to Atlanta yesterday was the worst! Bad consumer experience, Delta people treat you in a rude way, very stupid, screaming with people to organize queues. I had a problem with a flight attendent that was so aggressive on the way she was speaking, putting me in an embarassing situation.

We arrived in Atlanta Airport at 10am from XNA. After eating our meal, my wife and I fell asleep at our designated gate. When she woke, we checked and no notifications had come to the Delta app, her text messages or normal email address. I happened to look at the gate designation and then check the board for an update and noticed the gates had been changed. Since we were in Concourse B and the new gate was in A, we ran to the train made it to the gate with only 7 minutes to departure time to find the attendant on the phone laughing and obviously not on a work-related call. As she hung up, she said I've got to go deal with some rude customers. When we asked about boarding, she said they were done and offered no support for our next steps. My very frustrated wife was visibly upset and having been called rude called the attendant a name. In the ensuing incident, my wife made a gesture across the counter to see the attendant's name badge and asked for her name 3 times with no reply. At the customer service desk, we were flagged and looked at as hostile passengers from then on. We spoke with 3 different people before finding out Atlanta policemen had been called to arrest my wife for assault. Our trip to drop our son off in DC for a leadership conference has been ruined due to this company and it's horrendous customer service environment. The worst part in my mind was instead of offering help to weary, paying customers this entire staff proceeded to have my wife arrested and jailed rather than helping us find a solution for our travel dilemmas and obvious frustrations.

Our flight was significantly delayed and ultimately cancelled, causing considerable emotional and financial damage. Nine of us have planned to attend my son's master's degree graduation at Georgia Tech in Atlanta for months. He was diagnosed with autism when he was young, and it's an incredible accomplishment for him to be able to work and complete this degree from a prestigious school, Georgia Tech, in computer science. Seven of us from Sacramento and two from Iowa were heading down to Atlanta to stay there on Friday, including my 102-year-old father. Additionally, I have arranged to hold a seminar in Atlanta for my business, which has required months of planning for the venue, marketing, and other details. Two of us arrived in Atlanta on time with Delta from Iowa. But the 7 of us with the flight Sacramento to Atlanta – DL 904 - May 2, 12:11 PM–7:41 PM were delayed repeatedly and lied to from 11 am to 11:30 pm, and finally they canceled the flight.  I filed a complaint with Delta, and they issued a $150 voucher and a refund of $88.36. We have suffered a financial loss of several thousand dollars, not to mention the emotional loss. We used Delta exclusively for National and International flights. No more!

So, we paid $225 per person to upgrade to Comfort +. Not worth it. Especially on this return to US trip. At some airports, like in Nice, France, Delta isn’t really running the show. Thus, priority boarding doesn’t really happen. No one is calling your group, so groups “after” you board ahead of you. Free for all. The other promise: complimentary drinks and snacks. Few drinks were offered with no explanation to how to procure more. (Do it yourself. Go the the galley in the back of the plane.) you are flattered at online checkin the day before. Here’s your entree options, please choose. Then find out it’s the very same 2 options everyone $225 behind you are also getting. Only difference is they asked you ahead of time. The seat space increase was not that significant enough for us to note anything remarkable. We also traveled light so dedicated overhead bin space wasn’t useful. In short - round trip about $900 extra paid, didn’t give us much of anything extra. Won’t waste our money again on Comfort+

Every time I fly with this airline, it’s a terrible experience. There’s about a 90% chance my flight will be delayed, and it’s become almost expected at this point. It doesn’t matter what time of day I fly or what airport I’m departing from—it’s always the same story: delay after delay. The most frustrating part is that customer service never acknowledges the issue. Not once have I received a genuine apology or even a simple explanation. Instead, I’m met with vague excuses or complete silence. It’s as if they don’t care about the inconvenience or disruption they cause to people’s schedules. I’ve missed important meetings, lost hours of sleep, and spent countless dollars rearranging plans because of their consistent lack of reliability. When I try to bring up the issue, they either brush it off or act like it’s a one-time occurrence—which it absolutely isn’t. The lack of accountability is infuriating. I understand that delays happen, but when it becomes the norm, something needs to change. I’ve flown many airlines, but none come close to being as consistently unreliable as this one. Until they start taking responsibility and making improvements, I’ll avoid flying with them whenever I can.

First time on Delta in some 20 years and it will be the last. I was lured by the promise of flying the A330Neo but on both the JFK to ATH and then back to JFK ended up on a 19 year old A330 that must have been acquired from a defunct Latin American airline. Why did I guess this? The inflight navigation was solely in Spanish, the aircraft had ashtrays in the lavatories, only one of which was functioning on the ATH-JFK segment. The lie flat seats were beat and, this was new to me, there were flying insects in my window, a few of which got out and wandered about. Really Delta? Cabin crew staff was efficient and pleasant. I ordered a "special" lamb dinner which I handed to my partner who ended up very very ill. We asked for the proverbial "barf bag" of which there were none so she was handed a large garbage bag. The purser was kind and promised to make a note to Headquarters ... its been a month and we don't expect any communication. Let me add that my non-special dinner was inedible but my meal from JFK was good. Lesson learned Delta!

I was on my way back home to Los Angeles from Cancun. Not only my airfare was more expensive than other airlines such as Alaska and Aeromexico, the service was just really so so. There are no meal provided to and from Los Angeles and the flights were right at meal time. I kept thinking what services they have provided that deserve higher airfare, but I don’t find any. Even other cheaper airline provides free WiFi on board as well. On top of that, they subject their passengers to a “tsa bag search” at the boarding gate, where they randomly selected passengers and forced them to a bag search. My friend who took another airlines told me they don’t see such search happening at their gates. And I wonder how “randomly” those passengers were selected. All were traveling alone, and minority. Not only the staff asked me to take my carry on baggage to check stations in a stern voice as if I’ve done something wrong, I had to open the bag for the staff to examine. I then have to take off my shoes and let them check my shoes and socks. All these happened at the gate at a slow speed while people kept passing by and watching. I passed the check of course but I’ll never take Delta again. Save some money and save the trouble. By the was, when I asked the flight attendant what drinks or food they have, she told me to check the menu. I mean, does that mean they have everything on the “menu”? They don’t even have almonds which is my usual option.

If you like having your flights delayed, being lied to, incompetent employees and terrible customer service then Delta is for you. We arrived at 0530 for a flight to LAX for a cruise. They were so unequipped, the didn’t check the maintenance of their aircraft, chemicals used to de ice the wings and will not give you a refund. We boarded and de-boarded three different planes. The first one didn’t have de-icing for the wings, the second one sat on the tarmac for two hours and was taken to the hanger for maintenance and the final one didn’t have fuel and needed over 10000 gallons to be filled. My family tried to get off and they refused because they had shut the door. We missed our paid for cruise due to their lies, negligence, incompetent management and delay after delay after delay. Delta employees, including the pilot, told us we would make it in time for the cruise. We sat for over 9 hours, our cruise left at 3:30 and we touched down at 3:30. They gave us half of our ticket price, a hotel that was completely booked because of all the delays and the only reason they gave us the vouchers was because it was maintenance problems. If it is the weather causing delay they don't give you anything. We had to pay 400 dollars to fly out, rent a van for 130 for two days of use, buy our own hotel rooms because the one they gave us didn’t have any extra rooms. Today we fly out, scheduled to depart at 7 o'clock and the expected departure is 2300.

This is the first time I have flown Delta. This flight was scheduled to leave at 1 PM from JFK to Buffalo. It pulled back from the gate. It headed toward the runway, but was turned around and returned to the gate. After waiting 50 minutes with no explanation whatsoever, passengers started getting antsy and asking questions. we were told that there was a piece of equipment mandated by the FAA, that had to be obtained before the plane could leave. We waited an additional 30 minutes. Ultimately, they received a small item that was inconspicuously delivered to a passenger. As it turns out, it was a seatbelt extender. When originally pulling away from the gate, they only had one on board. This particular passenger required a second one. That is what we waited an hour and a half for. It is understood that every person deserves dignity, it is ridiculous to have delayed 90 people a total of two hours, awaiting a seatbelt extender. The passenger requiring this should have been deboarded and placed on the next flight with the appropriate equipment. Additionally, what was most upsetting about this was the deceit. The seatbelt extender was. delivered to the passenger very secretly. The minute it was delivered, we were backed out of the jetway and in a queue of 12 planes to takeoff. Altogether, the delay was 2 to 2 1/2 hours. The flight was a 50 minute flight.

Delta left us stranded in the Jacksonville Airport for 52 hours and still couldn't get a flight out. We booked with American Airlines and got out of there. We submitted a refund to Delta for the flight they cancelled and could not get us rebooked and they DENIED the claim! Delta Airlines is the Worst Airline in America! Don't Fly Delta!

Hurricane/tropical storm Debby was in the projected flight path of our upcoming flight, so i went up to the desk to see what to expect; if our flight was still a go, take longer because they’re going to fly around, ext. Crew member sees me, rolls his eyes, and after i ask him for a flight update due to storm he says “do i look like a weather station?” Then at check in, i have my hands full, watching my child, and we have 3 tickets. He took & scanned the tickets of people before me, but tells me “lets be self sufficient” and makes me scan the tickets myself, then turns to spouse behind me and says “you got a problem?” I cannot express how purposively this crew member was trying to make us start an altercation. He also did that fake “i appreciate you” statement with the most condescending tone, i did an audible mmmmmm back, and he started saying things behind my back, not loud enough that i could hear it, but loud enough to know he was being rude. It was awful to have a person use their job authority to be maliciously hostile knowing they will have no repercussions. we wont be flying with Delta ever again.

While trying to board the plane in Atlanta at the gate, 2 employees traumatized the entire group of customers and bullied and abused at least 10 customers, one of them being myself and my friend. One of the female Delta staff members began by yelling at everyone impatiently on the intercom repeatedly. She was angry, and was not able to maintain any semblance of professionalism. She specifically picked me out of the line while waiting to board, and separated me from my friend I was traveling with. She proceeded to allow my friend to board and then when I stepped up behind her to board, this person screamed at me to get back in line and she refused to allow me to board. I waited in disbelief and then she sent me to the other agent who asked me what was going on, I said I didn't know but she won't let me board, at this time the agent yelled across the entire crowd, "Stop causing drama lady!" Instead of the agent scanning and letting me board she sent me back to the agent who refused to board me and threw me out of the line. I stood there observing her yelling at other customers, trying to separate kids from their families, another customer experiencing exactly what they just did to me but she was screaming. At this point my friend stared videoing the agents with her cell phone of all the bullying going on, the agent saw her and stomped over and forced her around the corner out of anyones view in the boarding cart and it was at this time that the agent grabbed my friends phone from her hand and erased all her video footage. She proceeded to yell at, tower over my friend, and repeatedly threaten her that we are not getting on the plane. My friend repeatedly told her that she was terrifying her! It continued and the agent came back to me and the threats continued that "they" are reviewing the video to determine if "they" are going to let me board. She then proceeded to gaslight me saying she wasn't the one not letting me board and that I may be not able to board. Why? I have no idea. After the entire plane was boarded, I was finally scanned and let on, the last to board, my friend was shaking and totally traumatized from the assault and abuse. After I got home I wrote a detailed complaint, letters to the board members in administration and received Zero response from anyone but they stated that they did an investigation and nothing happened, no one remembers me, they lied and said there was nothing on the video (which was over 30 minutes of dozens of incidences of abuses towards customers), refused to accept any responsibility. Delta absolutely covered up this ordeal with an entire gate being traumatized and abused by 2 of their employees and then my friend assaulted with her phone being grabbed from her hand and her videos being deleted.

As an expat American living abroad, it was nice to be on Delta for my short vacation to Mexico City from Asia. Check-in and security was a breeze. In the air, Delta's hard product stood out and was industry competitive with generous seat width and pitch in Premium Economy, good meals, and the most eclectic offering of feature films, tv shows and wi-fi capabilities. I liked their artisan Mexican 'Someone Somewhere' amenity kit, which had the most comfy eye mask I've worn. Transiting at ATL airport to MEX was ok albeit lacking in amenities compared to some airports in Asia. Delta really shined most for me on my connecting flight when it came to my interactions with the cabin crew. I want to give a shout out to the cabin crew (Sam and Jerry) on DL 587 to MEX where I got a chance to have a chat with them about my travels, living abroad and life as a Delta flight attendant. It was a breath of fresh air as their candor, approachability and unserious friendliness demonstrated to me what makes American hospitality and how much I missed home. Delta continues to be my favorite American airline.

The service was good and not what I have an issue with. The reason I would give them zero stars if I could is that I paid about $2,000 for a ticket (not a basic economy ticket) that was booked directly with Delta and the airline has not awarded me SkyMiles for this flight. I have contacted customer service several times and get no answer as to why the SkyMiles have not posted. Customer service then gives me the run around and the issue does not get resolved. As a result I will be canceling my Delta Amex and will be choosing to fly a more competent airline in the future after draining my SkyMiles account.

Weather cancelled my flight initially. Which I accepted, for sure. They rebooked it, and weather made another delay. I was again, frustrated but okay. But then after the initial delay from 6:30 am to 2 PM, the plane is here and well, but they say the flight crew is stuck in traffic and entirely missing. They were not even past the security checkpoint. Everyone sat there at the gate with the plane ready for a solid 40, 50 minutes. We ended up leaving the gate at 3:45 PM.  I had a connection flight that I could have made without a problem if it were not for that "crew missing" comedy. Now is that delay "weather"? Dont try to play with your words and avoid responsibility. For me, never again. With the extra money they charge you, you would expect their system to be more organized and better customer service. No service. no hotel, no rides, nothing. What's the point of paying extra when you receive the same nonexistent service as any cheaper airlines? Just avoid it. Even Spirit gave me vouchers for non mechanical problems.

Terrible service, they have been moving my flight around since 2020! I originally had a flight for a ski trip right before Covid, they canceled my flight and gave me e credit up to that point everting was still ok, after that I use the credit for a flight I needed to New York, it had a layover in Atlanta but I didn’t think much of it, they confirmed it. A month before my flight I get an email saying that they had changed my first flight and due to this I would not be able to make it to my layover flight to New York so I canceled it and had to book a new flight with a different company, now they are saying that I never payed my original flight all the way back in 2020 and refuse to give me the credit even tho I have several conversation with representatives that clearly show I had paid and confirmed my flights.

Horrible service and attitude. I was scheduled to fly from Toronto to Atlanta on November 20, 2024, at 6:15 PM with flight DL2714, and from ATL to Melbourne FL with flight DL2435 at 10:39PM. I was boarding zone 8 and at the gate the Delta agent announced that the travelers can check the carry-on for free. I had a connecting flight in Atlanta, and I decided to keep my carry-on and went boarding. When approached the plane entrance, Delta male flight attendant told me that he won’t let me in the plane with carry-on. I tried to explain the reason I need my carry-on with me, but he called another person and that person laid a hand on me by pushing me away. I asked him to stop it and return to the gate to check my carry-on. After I checked my carry-on and returned to the plane the same male Delta flight attendant refused to let me in the plane and threaten me by calling security. I missed my two flights and was stressed. The Delta flight attendant was very rude and demonstrated a horrible attitude.

I’m writing this review for my 90 year old mother. She took a first class flight from Lisbon to Norfolk Va. her flight was diverted back to JFK. When she arrived she along with 3 other senior citizens waited for wheelchairs that never came. All of Deltas staff adandonded them leaving them. She was forced to walk through JFK and sleep on the floor. At 5am when someone finally got to the desk. My mother asked again for a wheelchair and to be taken to the lounge. Again no wheelchair appeared. She then had to sleep on the floor until the flight which she had to walk again. Two one hour calls to delta later. Still no answers. This is elder abuse. And they should be prosecuted. It has now been 12 days and no response from delta. Absolutely unacceptable and disgusting. I only hope their mothers never have to endure such horrible abuse.

I have flown Delta on a round trip from SFO to Accra twice now, and for some reason, there is never Wi-Fi on the flight from JFK to Accra and vice versa. Every time, it’s attributed to a "technical issue." However, the flight from SFO to JFK consistently has Wi-Fi without any problems. Currently, I’m at JFK waiting for my flight to ACC, and I just received a notification that there will once again be no Wi-Fi on this leg of my journey. As a loyal Delta customer and SkyMiles member who frequently flies within the U.S., I’ve never experienced this issue on domestic flights. This repeated lack of service on the Accra route raises serious concerns about selective treatment of certain destinations and passengers. It almost suggests that some routes and individuals are prioritized over others, despite everyone paying the same—if not more—for their tickets. This is unacceptable and not reflective of the equitable service Delta should provide to all its passengers, regardless of their destination. I urge Delta to address this inconsistency and rectify the situation immediately. Every customer deserves the same quality of service. Do better, Delta!

June 30/24, second flight Bogota-Atlanta, Delta 983. The plane landed in Atlanta early, but it took them about an hour to open the door. I was very worried because I didn't want to miss my connecting flight to Greenville, SC. ​​I saw a couple with a baby pass by my seat looking for the exit and I went after them. At that moment, one of the flight attendants arrives and yells at me loudly and in a disrespectful tone, "Ma'am! Where do you think you're going?" He took my suitcase from me, I explained the situation to him, but he continued to be furious and yelled at me to go to your seat and something else that I don't remember. I was in row 28, in row 26 there was a lady, who I believe works for the airline, because 80% of the time during the flight she was standing and talking and laughing with the flight attendants. When I passed my seat, the lady also in a contemptuous tone yelled at me "where do you think you're going, mommy!" and I replied, "I think you look prettier when you're quiet," and I continued to my seat. The lady in seat 26 and those in row 27 began to make fun of me for the situation. I didn't say anything. I'm not a person who fights, and I'm also a person over 67 years old. You can't imagine how bad I felt as a person, being the laughing stock of so many people, because of the disrespect of an employee of your company. I never want to fly again with your company, I can't understand what training your employees have, what's going on with human resources?

Was noted on 2.5 hrs prior to flight departure that plane had been delayed 30 minutes without an explanation. Our connecting flight was only 1 hr reducing it 30 minutes. We later landed late for our connecting flight resulting in a missed connection by multiple passengers. Airline offered a flight 18 hours later without any accommodations, coverage of meals, etc. Deltas only solution was a $50 voucher for each passenger for a future flight. Why would I fly with a company that can’t get it right the first several times? After talking with other passengers who had missed their connection, we found out this is a daily occurrence on this particular route as they and us had experienced the exact same thing on our outbound flight just didn’t miss our connection as we were waiting on a pilot.

I would recommend avoiding Delta if at all possible. We had booked to fly from Atlanta to Dublin but ended up having our flight delayed 2+ hours. During the delay, the Delta desk agents would not answer any questions about the cause of the delay or how long the delay would be. The staff overall seemed to not know what was going on and even later stated that they "needed to go see what was going on" before they would be able to provide a departure time. Overall, the impression is of a poorly run airline, with staff that were unable or unwilling to answer any questions.

Flight was cancelled due to “construction” at MSP airport. It’s not like construction just happened overnight. This was something Delta could have avoided. Spent the night in the airport. Never again will I fly Delta.

Considering my review below, this was surprising. Mediocre at best. Seat etc was fine but so much was not. To start, some( not all) of the crew was grouchy or impersonal. A few were great. Second, at the gate free fast Wi-Fi was announced, neither was true. Once we crossed the coast line wi fi shut off for the rest of the trip.The crew insisted that was normal but considering on the same flight(reverse direction) same type aircraft we had full service, that just wasn’t true. One crew member quie2tly agreed and didn’t understand. In any case at least half the fee should be refunded (good luck to me.) The food was mediocre at best.In between the meal and snack only chips and cookies were available (10 1/2 hour trip) IFE was fine. I guess real complaint was a majority of crew who wanted to be anywhere else.
Terrible experience! The flight was late 2 hours and 20 minutes. In Atlanta we were running to catch their flight to Amsterdam. Worst company ever.

I am writing this review on our return flight back to NC from Arizona. We are in first class, seats 5A and 5B. Same seats we had on the initial flight to Tucson…but different airbus. We have experienced a broken tray, cold temperatures, horrible food, and on this flight, very mediocre flight attendant service. On both flights, by the time they got back to our row (5 - which is the last row in first class) there was no choice for “dinner”. We were stuck (both flights) with the fruit & Cheese box. It was supposed to have grapes - but did not…on either flight. The three tiny slices of apple were old and not cold nor crisp, some dehydrated apricots, a very few almonds that had lost most of their crunch, half of a small hard boiled egg, some rubbery cheese, and four crackers. If that is their idea of dinner, they are sadly lacking. Delta Airlines is cheap! Pathetic, yet expensive, experience! We chose Delta this time just to compare them with American, since those are the only two airlines that fly out of our small city. We will not fly Delta again. It is back to American Airlines for us!

Excellent experience except for the dinner which was fine no wow. Overnight flight, lie flat seat. Extremely comfortable flight made better by an attentive, friendly cabin crew. On time departure and early arrival. Entertainment was great. Literally nothing to complain about. Solid experience.

Rude staff experience with Sergio! On a Flight from FLL to LAX, Nov 18 2024 on 7:40. No seat assigned 2 days before Flight. No place for the carry-on and they over staffed with other people’s luggage. When I tried explaining to a team member called Sergio that I had to bring my luggage in as that's why I choose Delta, he was so rude and immediately threatened me saying he'll re-assign me to a different Flight. Then when I tried explaining I have a buissnes meeting I literally heard him copycat my words to a other team member saying "business meeting ahh". So rude. I'd never fly with them again. I'd take Spirit, Jetblue or Frontier before this experience any given day.

I will never fly with Delta again! They have zero customer service and will not help you in any way even though they are at fault. Every employee we talked to was completely rude and would not help us with our situation. First, they made us check our carry ons because there was no over head space even though they was plenty when we boarded the plane, then our flight was delayed from landing causing us to miss our connecting flight, our connection had at least 15 people that missed that flight just like us and they ended up leaving early which to me makes no sense. Every employee at the help desk was either rude or didn’t want to help us making us run around the airport to get any type of help. They wouldn’t give us a hotel and we couldn’t get a flight to our final destination till the next night causing us to miss a whole day from our trip. Then on top of everything they lost our luggage, one ended up in Tampa while the other stayed in Atlanta so not only were we stranded in a city with no place to sleep but we also had no clothes or really anything. It seems like every Delta employee goes through the same training of how to screw their customers over and be an extremely rude and disrespectful person. I have never been so upset with how an airline handled a situation before and Delta has lost my business for good.

This airline is terrible. They refused to turn on the air conditioning while they made us wait for 40 minutes in the miserable cabin. Not only did they not turn on the air one of the flight attendants went onto the overhead speaker and yelled at us for asking. They have not offered water either. They also sold me a ticket in which it said I would be able to have a carry on. They ended up making me check it, they are making me go to the baggage claim to get it, and there was no compensation. This is a miserable airline and I would not recommend this to anyone.

Flew out Dec 26 at 8:25. I was sitting next to someone with bad body odor. My head started hurting but I didn’t have a drink to take my migraine medication. The flight attendants said flight was bumpy but when it clear they would begin beverage service. I walked to the back of airplane to go to the restroom and 2 flight attendants were laughing and loud. I went back to my seat and after a while the attendants passed out water in cups (no ice) and then came around with a serving tray full of hot coffee. I asked for a Coke Zero but it never came. Delta’s customer service has gone down hill in the last few years.

Mishandling of luggage cost me ££ to replace. I made a claim for a refund due to the broken lock which I could not open and they refused it due to ‘wear and tear’ but it most definitely was not. My other luggage perhaps did have some wear and tear but my main luggage was broken and deemed unusable for international trips. A few things inside my bag were also broken somehow even though they were in a solid heavy duty suitcase so tell me again how it was normal wear and tear. This was at the start of May, and they only just got back to me.

Total failure. Horrible customer service in the LAX airport. Stayed in the line for 6 hours. Supervisor Omar even scared me with calling police regardless that i was with little baby stand in all day in the line because they had delayed and nobody could tell me if my flight is changed to other or no i just wanted to know what to do And left the desk telling us to go to the line again (6 hour line). Worst experience ever. Under his supervision when there were 100 people waiting were working only 3-4 desks however there were more then 10. Not giving any concrete answer to any question what to do when to come. Stayed in separate 4 lanes no any support solved the issue. Only one guy helped us after all day staying in different lanes before even they didn't know where who should stand

Everything started well. Decent breakfast served immediately after take off by an efficient attendant. Following that there was no service at all. For over 4 hours there was not one offer of a drink or snacks. I guess if I had rung the call bell I could’ve got something but it was pretty surprising not to have a single walk through by the guy. When I went to the rest room he was snoozing on the jump seat. In addition no seat belt/ back check before landing. The guy in front of me had his seat reclined for touch down. So not just service, but safety issues. Not ok! Not an inexpensive ticket either.

I had a departure from Syracuse NY to Laguardia 8/2/24. Approx 5 hours before take off, a text of cancellation due to weather. Although was to be my 1st flight, I'm not stupid. I did notice I was one of maybe 15 seats taken. They claim weather related. Ok. Fine. Weather was iffy at best. I booked a bus for as much money being last minute. Fast forward to Saturday eve ..... another text, we regret to inform you your flight from Lag-Syr is canceled. Definitely not worth their time to fly just a couple of people because the weather was fine. I was near Lag Saturday night and even during the rain, planes were taking off constantly. Another hit in the pocket for a last minute bus to Syr. I will stay grounded and forever stick with the bus (Flixbus) or Amtrak, both of whom got me to my destinations.

We had an awful experience in our Delta flight (Flight No DL1502 from Atlanta to DTW - 8.55 PM on 3.8.24), the flight service was rotten, throughout the flight, the hostesses were not paying attention to the passengers, and to the cleanliness of the aircraft. My wife, put her stuff on the seat we noticed the seat was wet and her jacket and purse got wet, after talking to the hostess, the hostess ignored and moved away like nothing happened. In the entire flight, there were no beverages, not even water was served. I pressed the button for airhostess call, surprisingly no body showed up, it was very much annoying. When the luggage came out of the belt, it was not only wet from outside, but also some of the cloths were wet inside of the suitcase. It was a complete frustration and an intolerable experience in the whole duration of the flight. When I called the customer service, instead of realizing and reassuring me that this will not happen again, they threw 5000 FF points on me. Delta cares only for money not for customer service as they claim.

PROS: Not even a minute of delay. Cabin crew was very friendly. Clean nice trip. Excellent service and food. Worth the money! Arrived earlier than we scheduled. Cons: The endless walking to the gates. Old movies. The airport Delta staff in Boston’s check in on the 27th of March 2024. Checking in for JFK -> Greece. This male replying to our questions with “I don’t know” or pretending not to hear us (didn’t even respond to my hello). Although one of our luggages was underweight by 5 pounds, he wouldn’t give it back to us so we could put things from the 2nd suitcase which was 2 pounds overweight. It was standing right there next to him! I can’t he replied because I would have to weigh it again! I didn’t say a word. I regret it. I had to carry the suitcase away from the counter to stuff a jacket from it, in my back pack (to reduce those 2 pounds) cause I was blocking the line when no one was behind me waiting. I have never been spoken/treated to so rudely in my life and did not answer any of my questions on how to make my 100$ bill in change since he would not accept a 100$ bill. I hope more people make complaint’s about this fellow.

I was scheduled to fly from YYZ (Toronto International Airport) to Atlanta on November 20, 2024, at 6:15 PM with flight DL2714 to ATL (Atlanta International Airport), and from ATL to MLB (Melbourne-Orlando) in Melbourne FL with flight DL2435 at 10:39PM. I was boarding zone 8 and at the gate the Delta agent announced that the travelers can check the carry-on for free. I had a connecting flight in Atlanta, and I decided to keep my carry-on and went boarding. When approached the plane entrance, Delta male flight attendant told me that he won’t let me in the plane with carry-on. I tried to explain the reason I need my carry-on with me, but he called another person and that person laid a hand on me by pushing me away. I asked him to stop it and return to the gate to check my carry-on. After I checked my carry-on and returned to the plane the same male Delta flight attendant refused to let me in the plane and threaten me by calling security. I missed my two flights and was stressed. The Delta flight attendant was very rude and demonstrated a horrible attitude.

Delta have zero empathy for people with disabilities. Their "accessible" procedure is long and complicated and then they don't follow through on what they promise. We recently flew from Orlando to Amsterdam and requested assistance on the flight - they ignored our requests multiple times and we had a horrendous experience on-board the flight because of it.
