They discriminated against disabilities and not interested in my voice regarding this. Their customer care supervisor Jacob Costillo has not taken my complaint seriously, palming me off with pathetic copy-and-paste responses, saying the matter is closed. I have forwarded my complaints for the U.S Department of Transportation and implore that other people think before booking a flight with Delta.

Delta flight attendant is not up to their standard as to why I fly them. I was on DL 0694 on September 25, 2024. I have been flying only with Delta airlines first class for the last 20 years and have never encountered a problem as I did tonight, the flight attendant in first class Sadya decided to stop serving a drinks without reason. We were not belligerent. We were not loud. We were not disruptive. The only thing I can think of that we did wrong was I did not know how to open the bathroom door. For the past 20 years, I was 336 pounds and have never used a restroom on an airplane before, the fact that I did not know how to open the door was not surprising to me, but apparently it made her feel that she needed to cut off service to us. I have loved Delta for 20 years and her behavior is embarrassing and it should not be tolerated by Delta as to the standards that they keep for their flight attendants.

To be the first time flying with Delta I can say that I loved it! I had a layover of 4.5 hrs and asked to David Watson if it was possible to fly on the next flight at 12:30pm (it was 11am) and he made it possible! Me and my daughter landed in SJ Puerto Rico 2 hrs prior our plans. Thank you David and Delta for being that awesome with us. Love all the perks that come with flying with you too. We had an amazing first experience and are planing to have more!

The flight attendant took my carryon suitcase because they didn’t have enough space in the aircraft. It fit all of the size requirements. They didn’t explain what was going on and that I wouldn’t see my luggage again until my destination. (I had one layover.) They stuck a tag on it without my knowledge and took my suitcase without my permission.

Delta is the WORST airline I have ever had the experience of flying with and VERY unprofessional and unhelpful in fixing their own mistakes. I waited at my gate for 6 hours and they continuously delayed my flight and then cancelled it. I was then sent to the ticket desk where I was REFUSED a refund when I could not take the next flight out 2 days later. They were very rude and unhelpful and I have never in my 21 years of flying experienced anything of the sorts. I cannot believe the terrible customer service.

Smooth takeoff and landing, but when we hit turbulence, and the captain announced we would be landing, no flight attendants came to take any new trash or check if tray tables were down, so we’re were forced to keep two cups of water secure on a tray table as we landed. Additionally, our request light was on, and no flight attendant was seen at all. We left the flight without knowledge of the number of our baggage carousel.

I should have looked online for recent Delta incidents before I booked this flight. While the pilot nailed the landing on the RDU to ATL run, the fact that she did it with flaps that never closed completely after takeoff, which followed a faulty fueling that caused us to have a 1 hr delay. We could only fly at 2/3 normal speed and elevation due to the flaps which made us another hour late, and because the flaps were stuck they wouldn't open fully for landing, so we had to be diverted to an emergency, extra long runway.  They never let on to the passengers of course. But the attendants all took the rest of the day off due to stress issues after landing, and that was the flight crew for my connecting flight to ABQ. Which made that flight also delayed two hours, as we had to wait for a replacement flight crew to show up.  I'm not ever flying Delta again, that's for sure! It's either Delta as a whole, or Raleigh/Durham-Delta ground crews in particular, but just do a Google search. If it was possible to give zero stars for everything but the flight crew (who were superb) I would, but you can't submit the review with at least one star in each category. Flight crew got 4 out of 5 for lying to us about how dire our situation was. There's a way to give people truth without causing chaos. It's called training.

The last two winter vacations I have had multiple delta flights. Every single one has been delayed (7 total). Last break (January 2024) my 8 hour itinerary ended up taking 32 hours. The overnight voucher they gave me for food and a hotel only work for the hotel and not food. They did not cover transportation costs to the hotel either. This break (December 2024) I had flights from Orlando to Minneapolis to Portland. The flight to Minneapolis was delayed enough that I couldn’t catch my connecting flight. So I was rerouted through JFK. That flight was supposed to have an hour and fifteen minute layover. But that flight was also delayed. I landed 3 minutes before my next flight started boarding. We taxied for over 20 minutes before getting to the gate, idling multiple times in the process. I had to sprint roughly three quarters of a mile to just barely make my connecting flight. The only reason I made it was that flight also delayed. Once I get to the gate they require me to check my bag. When I asked if I could keep it, they said no and blamed me for not boarding at the proper time. Not only could I not do so because the previous flight was so delayed, but delta had not even given me an assigned seat yet for the flight, let alone a boarding group. So even if I was at the gate, I would not have been able to board in the “proper” group. In summary, I will gladly pay extra money to avoid Delta for the rest of my life. I generally enjoy flying, and this company has made me do a complete 180. Hopefully I will never have to deal with this level of incompetence again.

Flight from JAX to ATL on September 1, 2024, with a connecting flight in ATL to YUL, and a 38 minutes layover in ATL. JAX depart time was suppose to be 7:10 AM and arrive in ATL at 8:23 AM. Depart time in ATL was suppose to be at 9:01 AM and arrive in YUL at 11:39 AM. Due delays in JAX, we only arrived in ATL at 8:45 AM instead of 8:23 AM. With this being said, I missed my connecting flight from ATL to YUL because of delays in JAX and we also got stuck in ATL tarmac for another 10 minutes before the aircraft got connected to the aerobridge. While running through the aerobridge to catch my connecting flight, I was also preparing my boarding pass on my phone and there it says, "we are sorry you missed your flight" and the Delta was offering an alternative flight with a layover for 12 hours. It asked me to accept the proposal which I did not hoping that I'd find another earlier flight. To my disappointment, all the flights they had for ATL to YUL was from 9:00 PM with arrival time of 11:49 PM. After rechecking my phone again, Delta rebooked me for the 9:00 PM flight with arrival time of 11:49 in YUL. I didn't have a choice in this situation so I had to sit all day and long hours (12 hours in total) at the airport. Airport was so crowded and hot and humid and food was expensive. I will never fly Delta again and never take Atlanta airport for layover or connecting airport. This was my worst travel experience ever.

Eighth time leaving Boston for TPA this year. Not once did we leave on time. Logan is the worst airport I have been to, with Delta’s experience at this airport being the worst. I travel all over, and Logan is a horrible airport.

Will be leaving more reviews once I can get ticket information from the military. But for this trip, I had 5 bags I needed to take 3 of which I intended on checking. I am active duty Air Force. Because I was unable to furnish orders, despite being in official uniform and having an active duty military ID, the rep at check-in told me I would have to pay for each of my checked bags. This cost me $300 more than what I paid for the ticket. No other airline has ever asked for my orders even when I am not in official uniform. Additionally, the customer service rep was rude. I can handle a simple "our hands are tied" answer, but it was almost as if she was happy to tell me about the extra charge and that no military ID nor uniform made any difference to her. Upon reaching my gate, when in line to board with military personnel, the rep chastised me because one of my checked bags was "too big and too heavy" without touching it. Again, not a big deal if it were as simple as "sorry sir, that bag needs to be checked" but I was treated as though I were a child and should have known better. I saw several bags larger than mine after boarding the plane and checking my second carry-on. The flight itself was fine. But the customer service was so bad I will pay any amount extra to avoid the treatment I received when I flew with Delta.

I was denied usage of the bathroom. I asked if there was a bathroom at the front of the plane, which there clearly is and was told nope there's isn't one you can use.  I have always flown with Delta and have never had an issue before. Let alone an issue of being able to use the bathroom. Now it's personal because they have stopped no one else from using the restroom up front. Do better, flight DL736 seat 13B. Wrote while on the flight.

I can say that my experience compare to others airlines better ranked was very good. On board staff was attentive, business class very good and food services not that bad as read. I would definitely consider flying again with them.

Flight and gate attendants do not know their job. One of the flight attendants raised her voice at me completely unprofessional. Another attendant was useless, unwilling to help. After missing a flight earlier because a pilot didn't show up. Now the pilot is waiting for a late employee for 20 minutes.

I booked flights with my credit card points and only had enough points for economy class. The flight was full so I could not upgrade my seats or even select seats in advance. I was assigned a seat when I arrived in the exit row next to the door. I am somewhat over weight but not morbidly obese. My hips are not abnormally big. This seat was fully metal with no holes below the arms. I could barely squeeze into the seat. It was painful to sit in it. I told the flight attendant that I couldn’t fit and she said the flight was full so there were no other seats. I could not buckle the seat belt so I asked for an extender. She said that an extender could not be used for that seat. So she had to go around and ask the entire plane if someone would switch with me. Thankfully a nice couple in the back agreed to switch and my boyfriend and I had to make the walk of shame to the back while the flight attendant had to loudly notify the pilot to delay take off until we could be reseated. People were glaring at us as we walked back. I was mortified and spent half of the flight crying tears of humiliation. Normally the exit row seats afford passengers more room and larger people book them for that reason. I had no trouble fitting into the regular economy class seat and the seatbelt fit fine. Anyone over a size 12 would have struggled to fit in that seat and we all paid to sit in that section. It is not fair to any paying passenger to have less space than the other passengers in that section. Delta is the only airline that makes regular passengers wait until they arrive to receive seating assignments. I will not be purchasing tickets from this airline again.

It took 45 minutes to check our bags. We were late for our flight and left in Montreal. Then a representative threatened to not put me on a flight at all. No one from Delta was even around to ask for help after an hour. An airport rep had to find a Delta employee. Online and phone support was useless. It wasn't until I was back in the US did they even want want to help. Don't fly Delta! They ruined a great trip.

I had a wonderful experience on this short flight.A full meal was served by an efficient and friendly flight attendant. The entertainment, seat comfort, and service (including baggage delivery) were all excellent. Literally nothing to complain about. An unusual pleasant trip.
Horrible. First class flight for 2.5hrs. No tvs and frozen salads. Delta plays dumb and wants you to jump through hoops to get any compensation on 3 tickets.

The consistency of Delta up front needs to be recognized. My fourth flight in a week and despite the various lengths and aircraft the service was top notch. This particular plane has got to be the most comfortable narrow body around. Of course 2-2 seating but other than that generous legroom and wide enough seats. The food was quite good(all on one tray) the IFE was wonderful and the flight attendant serving our cabin mostly alone was so kind and extremely efficient. Wi fi is so inconsistent on this route and we are warned in an email that it might not be available yet it’s works perfectly and is free.

My luggage was lost during my trip from NY to Atlanta. The wrong label was attached when I had to book my luggage at departures due to the plane being too full to accommodate my hand luggage. The bag was found being sent to Nebraska but on its way back to Atlanta Delta once again lost my bag. I had to spend my whole vacation in USA without all of my personal belongings. Not impressed.

Excellent care for flying with baby. Secured aisle seating. Stewardess were exceptional. Very helpful and accommodating. Even offered a free flight change for following day since delayed. Very impressed with customer service.

Flight was delayed due to maintenance on a plane. Our flight was scheduled to board around 8:40 PM when I arrived at the terminal late I expected the plane to already be boarding. As I stood there about 10 to 20 minutes with all the other passengers waiting to get on the plane finally had an announcement for the agent stating that the client was having maintenance and they would be delayed boarding until 1020 and the flight will takeoff at 11. Once we finally started boarding the plane the 11 o’clock takeoff time came up past. Around 1110 captain came on the intercom and stated that they were working on the plane and they were waiting on the maintenance guy to get back so that Atlanta could work with him to verify the flight was ready to takeoff. About 20 minutes later came back on the intercom stating that maintenance was on the plane and they were going to be working with Atlanta. The pilots were gonna be stepping off because there was going to be a lot of noise in the cockpit would be to takeoff. A little after 12 o’clock, the captain came back on the intercom stating that we were going to have to board this plane. We should’ve never boarded this plane that we should’ve been put on another plane and he was very disgusted with the situation. Continue to say once he had more information, he would let us know what gate we were going to so that we could board a different plane to continue our destination in Buffalo. Around 12:30 AM we finally boarded a different plane and we left a little after 1 AM. When I arrived in Buffalo, the rental car centers were all closed and had to sit in the airport for 3 1/2 hours until they reopened. Due to this delay, my hotel stay for the night that I was billed for didn’t sleep in the bed because I had to continue on to work with my customer that day which now put me being up over 36 hours. I spoke with the Delta agent who told me I need to file a complaint stating what it happened and then I would be compensated to what went on. So I Submitted my complaint to Delta where an agent with an email address from Delta that states wecare.com. Which obviously they do not Delta gave me a 3000 point to my SkyMiles for compensation for what happened that day. So I’m out over $200 for my hotel stay. Lack of sleep because of having to continue to my Customer site without being able to sleep and work 36 hours straight cause of this. Was not able to get a rental car had to sit in the airport for 3 1/2 hours waiting on the rental car center to open because of this. And the supervisor from Delta stated that the best they could do is give me 3000 bones points for this mishap. That shows me the Delta should change their email to say we don’t care what happens to you. I can understand if it’s an act of God due to the weather. But this was an active Delta decided to do a maintenance on a plane that was scheduled for a flight. Confidence of Delta and their response to their customers because of this is ridiculous. I was told by the Delta agent joy that they do not Refund hotel stays that were previously booked. I don’t know most people who don’t previously book travel with the hotel and travel with a car rental center. To be told that the best they can compensate me was 3000 points was ridiculous and that there would be no more further contact with me because that was all they were going to do. I’m a Gold member without Delta. And also have their American Express reserve card and I’m going to be treated this way.

I just had most unpleasant experience with Delta. I took Delta 847 from JFK to Austin. After I passed the gate with my carryon luggage, my husband and son had been asked to check in their carryon luggage. I waited for them a bit. Later when I waited to get on the plane, a man came to me and asked if I took off the tag from the luggage. I insisted I was not asked to check in luggage, he still kept questioning me. He asked for my seat number and I thought at least he could verify I was telling the truth. The man just went ahead and generated a luggage tag and forced me to check in the carryon. Delta could keep testing customer sentiment like this, as for myself, I will never ever book another flight with Delta.

DL2996 early morning flight was cancelled only 8 hours prior departure. I found out via email upon waking up a few hours before the airport. There were no alternatives from/by Delta to get me to the wedding I planned to attend that day at destination. Both chat and phone agents were practically unreachable. Ultimately, I had to pay up a very significant amount and much more for a very last minute flight on another carrier out of my personal pocket. Delta provided zero compensation for this last minute cancellation.
Pretty much impossible to get service after the first hour on a 6hr flight! When I walked to the back to request a beverage I was told they'd be coming around, which they never did.

This travel was a nightmare. Due to technical issues with flight, staff started deplaning people because at the time of boarding, Flight captain said that there was problem with tyre. 1 hour later, staff informed that we will switch to another plane. We took off at 10 when plan was supposed to leave at 7:21 PM. I lost my next connecting flight, so had to stay in Atlanta overnight. Airline gave option for meal and motel, so kind of them but they don't have wisdom to understand that many people travel for business or work. And for them, meal voucher, motel is not priority but Time is priority.  Next day flight again delayed from 9:16 am to 10 Am. Great. Then that Front desk staff asked me to get my Duffle bag tagged because there is no space in cabin. I explained to him, that this is not hand carry, a duffle bag, can fit under my seat as its soft and can fit in little space. First of all, he acted stubborn, yelled at me, closed the entry door and called security on me because I am not giving my bag to tag, even I explained that I am scared that I will get my bag damaged. As 2 months ago, They mishandled my hand carry , broke wheels and damaged my stuff inside. I had to literally drag that heavy hand carry.  Flight attendants never had problem with me to put that bag under seat. What was the point of all this fuss.

Delayed flight twice before totally canceling it. I was bummed but understand it's related to the IT fiasco. What is not  related to the CrowdStrike problem is how Delta treated us! First, the gate agent announced the cancellation and then immediately left! Offered no help other than to say we should find a help desk to rebook. Stood in line for 3.5 hours only to have them tell me there are no fights for 4 days. When I asked to change my flight to 8/4, she told me no, they would only book within the week! I also couldn't get a hotel voucher because every hotel in the area is booked. Went to get my checked bag and after another hour in that line, we were all told that they would not be getting any more bags until 6:30am (this was now midnight) because they were too far behind with all the cancelled fights. After 5 more hours of waiting for them to get my bag, I was told they can't find it. They won't report it lost yet, they said they just can't find it and I need to wait longer! I can't believe they get away with treating customers like this. Delta used to be the only airline I trusted. I am shocked at their mishandling and complete lack of compassion. Oh wait, they did come through the line with water and animal crackers for us. Seriously Delta??? You should be ashamed!!!

While the flight was fine even in economy my complaint is that despite the warnings to check your seat for all your belongings before deplaning in Rapid City I was interrupted by a fellow passenger and forgot my bag containing my noise cancelling headphones ($40) in the seat. I believe the cleaning crew either at Rapid City airport or Minneapolis airport where the plane returned to simply found and kept my headphones. That is theft. I was specific about the appearance of the missing item (blue soft bag with noise cancelling headphones) and  the seat 6D. There is no way that the cleaning crew did not find it. The only other choice is if another passenger saw it and took it. I will not fly Delta again.

Although this was over a four hour flight in a wide body1-2-1 configuration it was clearly not Delta One. The differences being no club access, no pillow or blanket and a reduced meal service. Despite all this it was a pleasant experience which, for me, has included the typical pleasant, efficient cabin crew.The breakfast (pictured) was totally adequate and IFE excellent. Really no complaints except a blanket and pillow would have been nice for an over four hour trip with a lay flat seat. By the way I keep hearing about free Delta Wi-Fi and haven’t experienced it once.

I am writing to file a formal complaint regarding an incident that occurred on my recent Delta flight. The issue involves the behavior of a flight attendant as well as the conduct of the Minnesota ground staff and Delta supervisors. I was a passenger on flight 2987 from Philadelphia to Minnesota, with a connecting flight to Los Angeles (flight 748). Shortly after takeoff on flight 2987, my three-year-old daughter urgently needed to use the restroom. The nearest restroom was in the first-class section, so I began walking her there during mid flight about 20 mins after take off. However, a flight attendant stopped me and informed me that I could not take my daughter to that restroom. I explained that my daughter might have an accident in her seat, but the attendant insisted that we return to our seats, even when I asked if it would be acceptable for my daughter to have an accident. The attendant's response was affirmative, indicating it was acceptable. Reluctantly, I returned to my seat with my distressed and crying daughter. A few minutes later, I observed a man from first class get up and use the restroom without issue. At this point, I decided to return to the restroom with my daughter. I informed the flight attendant that her behavior was unacceptable and hypocritical. I then proceeded to use the restroom with my daughter. During this incident, I asked for the flight attendant's information, and her name which was denied. Upon landing, I sought the flight attendant's information and saw her speaking to the flight supervisor outside the door as we exited the aircraft. I stopped and asked the flight supervisor if we could discuss the situation away from the terminal to avoid blocking customers. He initially told me to wait and talk there, but I suggested we move to the front to avoid obstructing other passengers. The supervisor then insisted I go to the top and wait for him. Once I identified that the supervisor was not handling the situation professionally, I asked to speak to his superior. As I headed up, the flight attendant and I exchanged words during the situation. However, as I was leaving, the flight attendant continued her uncontrolled unprofessional comments and proceeded to clap her hands four times in a confrontational manner, as if she wanted to fight, in front of other flight attendants and supervisors. This behavior went unaddressed. This experience was incredibly frustrating and distressing for both my daughter and me. I believe the treatment we received was unreasonable and lacked basic empathy, especially considering the needs of a small child. As I waited, the day shift manager arrived and, in a rude manner, ignored me to speak with the flight attendant and her supervisor down below at the plane.  After waiting for about 10 minutes, the manager returned, and I tried explained the entire situation to her but of course she interrupted me the whole time. Without listening to my story or understanding what happened, she informed me that I was banned from Delta flights for my conduct and could not reschedule a flight with Delta and use another airline. Her decision was based solely on the accounts of the flight attendant and the unprofessional in flight supervisor. I requested to speak to her supervisor, but she claimed in a disrespectful tone that she was the only supervisor and that no one was higher than her.

In an effort to be fair to all of Deltas' customers (and potential customers ), I believe that they need to know how they treat their customers and how we were stranded by them. Our luggage was lost and severely damaged when it was eventually recovered and now they are refusing to make it right. I was given the runaround and eventually just blown off.

Platinum Medallion member. Wallet was stolen on a trip, and presented ticket and official ID. Was allowed in every airport lounge except Atlanta. Total lack of customer service, inconsistent practices and the workers looked as though they couldn’t care less. RUN. BAD AIRLINE

Questioning myself if I will fly Delta again! My flight schedule was to depart from Guatemala at 8:15AM as we were already in the waiting room one hour before the scheduled time, changed the departure to 1:00 PM that is almost 5 hours delayed, and 4 hours later the again changed the schedule to 2:00 PM. That is now 6 hours delayed and still with a question mark as the luggage is outside the plane. I complained to them over messages and they lied to me that it was for bad weather and an hour later another airline departed to LA with no delays. Later I convinced the to tell me the “truth” that it was for maintenance issues. Regardless I believe Delta has serious problems with their existing management as last week my niece had the same problem. Their agents or people responding are very polite trying to compensate for the bad experience but the root of this problem is coming from their managers.

Unproblematic check in and departure from the very nice Delta Terminal at Detroit. Excellent boarding process. Uneventful flight, including efficient service with a round of drinks and a little snack, actually far better than what meanwhile one gets from nearly all European “Full Service Airlines” when flying short- and medium haul.

Starting with check in at PBI, poor customer service provided by Anselm - no greeting, smile, thank you and no way was he making the smallest effort to accommodate my special requests. I felt like an inconvenience and bother to him, rather than a valued customer. No beverage service on the flight because “not enough time.” On the positive side, they didn’t charge for head phones or carry on. Just disappointed overall, nonetheless.

Flew NYC to Lisbon and back. Delta provides a surprisingly enjoyable experience: both flights were punctual, fine IFE with lots of fun movies, food and drinks were top notch, friendly crews always happy to provide an extra snack or drink, even an alcoholic one. Also, I fell and hurt my leg the day before my return flight and they let me check my carryon for free.

Comfortable seats, but nothing else. Upfront at premium price for a cup of coffee and a (modest) bag of cookies on a two hour flight on an early (7am) morning flight. Why is a breakfast not possible to serve? The most questionable was the lavatory, probably the most disgusting thing I have seen on a plane: dirty, old, and outworn. Comparisons would be here out of line, simply it was disgusting.

On December 23,2024 my husband and I flew on First class Delta Airlines flight number DL0466 from Las Vegas to Atlanta. We were absolutely surprised and disappointed by the bad and poor service we got during this flight. The journey start with the boarding, the 2 flight attendant who worked at the front of the airplane (first class) were busy chatting laughing and using them private phone instead being present at door greeting the first class passengers try to make any eye contact or just smile as they expected to do I believe. Since those 2 flight attendants were so busy with them private stuff we didn’t get any pre departure drinks or even get asked if we want something to drink besides the 2 little bottles of water placed on the chair tray. Unfortunately the meals service took so long but right after we got our meals. Finally the 2 flight attendants went to the galley closed the curtain behind them and hide there the entire flight! Since we were sitting at 1A and 1B we could hear them them laughing and talking loudly behind the closed curtain, they completely ignored my request for extra water and only after 6 times ringing and waiting almost 15 minutes for some attention. The flight attendant approached with bad attitude by asking what do you want, no smiling no say sorry or any explanation why I need to wait so long for water so i can eat my meal. Finally I got my water but they run back to Galley closing the curtains and continuing talking and laughing . Both food trays left and didn’t get picked only few minutes before we start the decent, when we deplane my husband asked the flight attendant why you are so upset this is Christmas. She just looked at him had nasty face and didn’t say anything or apologize for the bad service we got. You cannot imagine how we got upset getting this kind of low class service, flight attendants who being so rude, especially after buying full price first class for our anniversary. Very sad to see how Delta airlines trust and believe they signing and trust those flight attendants will be the face and the ambassadors of Delta Airlines in the sky. But they just being rude, lazy, and providing the most bad service and experience I ever got on one of my flights with Delta Airlines.

My boarding pass said my flight was supposed to be boarding at Gate B5a. But the departure gate was changed to B15 without any notification. I was consequently unaware of this change and missed my flight. The only notification was the departure screen which is barely readable as it is constantly changing. There were no PA announcements, no text messages, no phone calls, apparently no alerts to App holders, but I don’t fly frequently enough to justify it. My friend who helped me book the flight does, but he didn’t give me any warning either so I was unawares. At least they got me another flight, 4 hours later than my scheduled flight.

Before I reached the airport, I checked in through Delta’s website. The online check-in was successful and I got a digital boarding pass. Upon arrival at the airport, I checked with a Delta staff at the counter and was told that my digital boarding pass was good enough and no additional check-in was needed. I went through security check and all gates successfully. However, when I lined up to board the plane, I was told that my boarding pass didn’t work and asked me to wait. I waited for a long time until the very end, because no staff was paying attention to passengers who needed to be cleared up. Finally, they checked their system and said I didn’t check in. I told them I have checked in online, otherwise I would not have a boarding pass. They showed a very impatient attitude and kept repeating that I didn’t check in. I didn’t want to argue and asked if they could check me in. They kept giving that bizarre attitude and a look that I didn’t know what I was doing. Shouldn’t they feel sorry for what their customers experienced? I was annoyed by their attitude and frustrated how messy Delta’s systems were. The staff was impatient and their arrogant attitude was unacceptable. I finally scanned my boarding pass and complained “stupid airline system”. Then they pulled me back and threatened me that if I didn’t apologize then they would take me off that plane and had me wait longer for the next flight. I was very angry but I did want to get back home. So, I apologized to them. Then they threatened me further that if take my attitude to the plane or they would take me off the plane. Wow, I don’t even have the right to be angry, given the way I was treated? Delta, your system is completely messed up. As a customer, I didn’t do anything wrong by checking in online but I was accused by the staff of not being responsible for checking in. Your messy system is not your customers’ fault. If your system is not capable of doing online check-ins, then don’t even ask your customers to do that. The staff abused their authority to threaten customers, forced customers to apologize and even took away customers’ right to be angry.  After I boarded the flight, the gentleman who sat next to me also complained how their seats were messed up and the horrible attitude their family received from Delta’s staff because they had a child who needed special care. I was not given a single smile but were treated with disrespect, impatience and arrogance. I cannot believe this is the level of service Delta.

My flight got delayed up to 8 hours until 11:35pm and they're still not confirmed with the time. This was due to the absence of a pilot and an assistance team. They were unable to help or do anything about it but offer us a hotel to sleep in until tomorrow if they bump us on another flight. This is the worse airline ever.

I want to highlight the good things because the good ones are more. I was flying back home to Washington, D.C., from Austin, TX. Due to some issues, the first flight was delayed. There was no way that I would make my connection in Detroit. I was devastated as I needed to get home as soon as possible. I kept looking for other flights, but my missed connection was the night's last flight to IAD. I would have to wait until the morning for the next flight. Unexpectedly, a kind flight attendant approached me on the plane and said, “Hey, I noticed that you missed your connection to Dulles Airport, but I found this delayed flight heading to Reagan Airport. If you don’t mind changing airports, you can be in DC tonight instead of tomorrow morning. Is this something you would like to do?” I immediately said YES! I needed to get home right away, so arriving home 40 minutes after my original flight was much better than waiting until the morning. Upon arrival at Reagan National, I checked with Delta about my luggage. As expected, they were delayed. However, they assured me that my suitcases would be delivered the next day to my house, and they kept their word! What a relief! With everything going on, the last thing I needed was to go to the airport to retrieve my things. And I am so thankful for that. What this flight attendant doesn’t know (because I didn’t ask for a flight change or even mention my situation to anyone. This was just amazing customer service is that she helped me get home on time to be there the last night with my very special recently adopted cat. See, he was special because he was the sweetest cat I’ve ever met; he only wanted to be on my lap all the time and to be petted nonstop. He was blind, and he was my friend’s cat, who unexpectedly passed away in April this year, and I took in my sweet baby. He got very sick and was getting worse by the hour. He was waiting for me to get home. I got home at midnight, and he crossed the bridge Thursday afternoon. Thanks to this kind soul and her outstanding customer service, I was there for him, keeping him company during his last night. And for that, I will thank Delta Airlines forever. From the bottom of my broken heart.

This is the 6th time where this airline hasn't given out no water, no food,.no nothing. Now we are sitting on this hot air plane waiting on the runway with no head phones to even watch a movie. This is the last time I'm flying Delta.

I flew from Zurich to Nashville and return during June. The flight is over 10 hours. On the flight to Atlanta we were fed one very small meal at the start of the flight and then an inedible soggy, partially cooked pizza pocket towards the end of the flight. The flight left late and on arrival we spent an hour getting through security to transfer to our Nashville flight. We had one tiny bowl of pasta in over 13 hours. This was a day time flight and was sold as a full service flight, yet we were hardly fed. Delta should advise economy passengers to bring their own food.  The return flight was an overnight flight so the paucity of food did not affect us so much. But on this flight my husband's entertainment screen did not work, although one staff member did try to rectify this. The lady who checked us in in Nashville was slovenly and unkept. Her hair was all over the place and not brushed and her clothes were too small and not ironed. Generally, the flight staff were badly presented (sadly, the uniform does them no favours), rude and appeared to have no pride in their work. They just shoved the meals at passengers and gave the impression that we were an inconvenience. I have travelled on many airlines and have never seen such scruffy and grumpy flight attendants. In contrast, the gentleman who checked us in at Zurich Airport was professional, well presented, helpful and friendly.

Terrible experience with Delta flights. Flight at BUF delayed for an hour and connecting flight at Atlanta missed. Delta did not provided any hotel at night and stayed in airport sitting all night and wait for next day flight at 8:30 AM. Again this flight again delayed and started at 10:00 AM. I was so upset and will never fly with Delta in my life.

Montreal to LaGuardia, the flight was on time, really small aircraft. As I entered the plane, by default I spoke in French (Montrealer) and the crew member very aggressively told me "No French, English Only", I realized my mistake and apologies and asked a question in English. Crew served only water upon request. LaGuardia to Miami, the flight was full, cabin crew was extraordinarily nice and smiley. IFE was available. Entire flight, the turbulence was pretty bad so crew wasn't able to run drinks and snack service and they were constantly very sorry about the situation. Overall decent experience, better than Air Canada, at least Delta serves something. Though It would have been nice to have bilingual crew on-board on flights to/from Montreal.

From start to finish including everything in between this flight was perfection. The comfort, the food, which was actually delicious for a change, the IFE, but most importantly the crew. Literally the entire cabin staff was genuinely gracious and efficient in every manner. I observed many of their interactions with passengers and each other and flawless is the word. I was happily compelled to tell as many as I could how impressed and grateful I was. I rarely complain and mostly expect things not to be up to par so this was a welcome surprise.Generally my Delta cabin crew experiences are good but this was perfect.

Flight from Munich to Atlanta in a retrofitted 767. a bit of a narrow seat for business class but good enough for an afternoon nap. Service was outstandingly friendly on the first flight, with good food and drinks options. Purser was always present with a personal word, informing each business class passenger about details such as arrival and departure gate for connecting, which is a great thing, knowing how huge Atlanta‘s airport is. Onward flight on a pretty old 757, which definitely did see better days. Though flight was 3+ hour, with no need whatsoever dinner was rushed. Limited choice of food, actually only one option was available when I was asked (was seated on 1B). And be careful, if you switch sort of drink, you get the different drink in a plastic cup, as they load (purser told me) one real glass per passenger on such „short flights“ (3,5 hr was the flight). So, yes intercontinental Delta was absolutely worth it, but on the medium range part of the journey I was really disappointed.

Cancelled my connecting flight due to the cyberstrike issues and made me wait in a 4 hour line just to tell me at the front that I needed to scan a QR code to be put in their virtual line, where they actually let people in from. So waiting 4 hours in line meant nothing because I didn't scan their QR code since they didn't have any staff making anyone aware in line that there was a QR code that needed to be scanned. Worst customer service, all the employees were unhelpful and unempathetic, out of hotel vouchers and employees telling mothers in front of me that their children will have to sleep on the ground of the airport.

Flew roundtrip from DFW-BOS. Both inbound and outbound legs were on A220-100. This is an awesome aircraft very similar to the A220-300. The engines are very quiet and the seats wider than bigger single aisle planes and very comfortable. Overhead bins large enough to accommodate carry on bags. Bathrooms are very spacious more so than on larger B737 or A320s. No delay inbound to BOS; however the outbound flight to DFW was delayed by 2h 30m because of the winter storms. Delta through its mobile app and messaging kept us informed of each and every flight delay update. Although delayed we were glad to be home rather than being stranded in BOS. WiFi worked flawlessly on both flights. Cabin crews were very friendly offering snacks and water periodically throughout the flight. The DFW-BOS nonstop is a new route for Delta and I would definitely fly Delta again.

Our flight #WS8209 (operated by Delta) was scheduled to leave Fort Lauderdale for Atlanta at 1:34 pm. It was announced that it was delayed 10 minutes, then another 30 minutes and finally cancelled - no reason given. I and my husband and 2 daughters scrambled to find another flight with no assistance from Delta or WestJet staff. After a few hours, we managed to find flights but not on the same plane. I complained via email to Customer Care, Delta Air Lines who offered as a "goodwill gesture" to email each of us a nominal credit voucher within 24 hours. Never happened. I sent several emails each being acknowledge by a different Customer Care Agent. Delta Airlines offer no compensation for cancelled flights, passenger inconvenience, etc. They accept no accountability for this and simply don't care. They just apoligize. Avoid using Delta Airlines.

We were almost at the airport for an international trip when I got a text from Delta saying they had canceled our flight and that they could not rebook us. They offered a credit on a future flight (!?!). This has never happened to me in 30+ years of international travel. We had to get to our destination that day. In the taxi, I was able to book tickets on another airline, leaving from another airport. Needless to say it was expensive to do so. Plus, getting from the first airport to the second meant a taxi fare of almost $200 USD. We phoned and insisted on a refund rather than the credit. The agent we spoke to did provide the refund. Nevertheless, I will not book with Delta again.

A really good experience. Because these are the old LATAM jets with 2-2-2 seating I was expecting a second rate trip. I was wrong. Seat was more comfortable than Delta Suites for sleeping. Crew was great. Warm and proactive service from start to finish. Skipped dinner 1130pm departure) so I  could sleep but breakfast 1 1/2 before landing was quite good. Didn’t watch anything but wi fi was great. Arrived early, everything was perfect.

I haven’t even gone in my trip and I have had numerous issues, starting with a cancelled flight due to “inclement weather” when multiple other airlines were able to fly to my destination on the same day. Delta did nothing to get me on one of those flights. In addition, I have had to call the airline FIVE separate times to correct issues or make changes because their website wasn’t working. This has put me on hold for up to 2 hours trying to do something as simple as change a seat or ensure my pre-check number was added (an issues they never resolved). Choose any other airline and you will have a better experience.

So for starters they had more available seats but choose to stick us in the last row. I had no space to place my bookbag due to the pilot occupying the space. Then I wanted coffee but the lady had just ran out. It wasn't pleasant at all.

Trash airline, said we had a 5 minute delay to fix something turned into an hour. Then told to deplane after sitting an hour to have to wait for another half hour til a new plane comes. If you have a chance to fly anywhere else other than Delta, I would suggest it.

I've unfortunately already booked a couple more flights with this airline, but that will be the last time. I thought that Delta's standards had improved over the years, but I had the worst airline experience on September 29th at LAX's terminal 3. I got to the airport early and I had my dog in his carrier. I got stopped by a female employee who told me that my dog was too big for his carrier. This is not the case, he is a chihuahua mix, and I have flown with my dog many times with no issues on much longer flights. She did not offer any solutions, just told me I could not fly with the carrier I had. I got in line to talk to customer service to figure out what my options were, and she then ushered me over again with a manager. The manager told me I could only fly with my dog if I purchased a different carrier from Delta. While the manager went to get the carrier, I waited for 45-50 minutes and almost ran out of time to check my bag. When the manager came back, she had a carrier the same size if not smaller than the one I walked in with. I was flustered and just wanted to get on my flight since I was running out of time, and they charged me $75 for a cramped Delta-branded carrier. I was stressed, my dog was stressed, and I had to literally run through the airport, jostling my poor dog, to make my flight in time (5 minutes later and I would have missed it.) It felt like a weird, gross scam and it was the most stressed out I've ever been dealing with an airline before. I fly extremely frequently (6-12 roundtrip flights a year via various airlines) but Delta has lost my business for good.

I flew from Toronto to Ft. Lauderdale on February 19th and was scheduled to return to Toronto on February 22 via Atlanta. That day Toronto was set to experience a snowstorm so I contacted Delta to inquire as to the status of my flight. I was put on hold and greeted by a sympathetic agent who could not help me. She immediately transferred me to a more senior agent who was very helpful, re-booking my flight for February 24 via La Guardia. On February 24 as we were about to board our flight we were told the flight would be delayed due to high winds in New York. I approached the agent for assistance as this delay would cause me to miss my connector flight to Toronto. She gave me the option to wait for further information and hope to get on a later flight standby or re-book another flight. Unfortunately, due to it being the Spring Break week, all flights were sold out and overbooked however she kept trying and eventually was able to offer me a flight at another airport February 26th. Again, I rebooked. On February 26th the scene at the airport was one of chaos due to holiday travel. The agents were most helpful and accommodating. On my flight from Atlanta to Toronto, again the flight crews and desk agents were so helpful and accommodating. I felt appreciated to be their customer and I felt they listened and wanted to help me! I want to thank Delta for their amazing customer service!

My husband and I have always placed Delta as the best of the best airlines ever! We do not believe that anymore. Our travel agent booked our trip in February 2023 for our upcoming thirty eighth anniversary in August 2023. We arrived early at Atlanta on August 14, 2023. We did everything we were suppose to do, then went to our gate and sat waiting an hour and 15 minutes to board (Gate E15) When they called for Comfort Plus we excitedly got in line to board our flight D1970. When the agent scanned my passport and ticket it would not go through, the agent, who had a horrible attitude asked, where are you flying to?! I stated St. Lucia, she said they changed the flight to boarding gate E10 and the plane has already left. My husband and I were furious because nothing was announced, nor was our name called to let us know of the last minute change. My husband attempted to ask the Delta Rep when did the flight change? She rudely stated, I don't know sir. All I know is the flight gate was changed to E10, they were to depart at 9:45 am, but they departed earlier. My husband asked, what time did they depart? She stated at 9:43 sir. Please move out of the line. We missed our flight. We went to the Delta desk and the agent was no better. We advised her of what had happened and she said, I don't see that your flight was changed to another gate? She then kept looking and said, oh yeah, at the last minute they changed it, and you and your wife are listed as a no show. By this time we are heartbroken. We asked if there was another flight going out? She responded not today. We called our travel agent and she got a Delta Rep on the line for Delta Vacations and she was very unsympathetic. Our travel agent kept asking what could we do to fix this? The agent stated for us to go back to the Delta desk and see if they could book us on an other flight. We again advised we were told there were no more flights going out. We went back to the Delta help desk and the agent had the nerve to say: I don't know why they sent you back to me. They can see the same thing I can. She repeated that statement six times before saying there was nothing she could do. We called for a shuttle to return us back to our hotel, having to pay another $167 plus tax. Our travel agent was able to get us booked on the next day flight August 15, 2023 (this is our anniversary day). She got a Delta Rep on the line to see what compensation could be done and every agent we spoke to was adamant nothing could be done because it was our fault we missed the plane. We ended up losing a whole day (August 14,2023) at the resort, and had to spend more money for a hotel stay. To say the very least we are extremely disappointed in the way things were handled with Delta and will be using American Airlines or another airline moving forward.

One of the worst services! Stay away! 9 pm: I had my flight at the time. I sat in the flight on the runway for 3 hours without takeoff, before it was returned to the same gate (due to weather conditions, so not Delta's fault). 12:00 am: Then, there was an announcement made by Delta to go to helpdesk for next steps. 2: 30 am: After a 2 hour line at the helpdesk, I was told that they can't help with international flights, and I should call customer service. Customer service has a wait time of 1.5 hours 4 am: I connect to customer service. After taking my information and everything, the person asks to transfer me to another team, with a wait time of 30 mins 4:30 am: The new person just repeats what I had already shared and without asking transfers me to another team. The wait time is 1.5 hours. Next morning: 10 am: Wait time is 2 hours. 12 pm: We are experiencing delays. If you'd want to continue, new wait time is 1.5 hours more. So, after spending an entire night (last 8 hours) trying to reschedule a flight that was canceled by Delta, I still couldn't get even a booking done! Next day, the poor service continues! One of the worst possible services!

My DELTA flight to London was delayed 4 TIMES because they couldn't find pilots. Then we boarded the plane at 12:45am and the pilots could not reach their INTERNAL Delta ground control to take off - the pilot announced this on the airplane as we waited on the plane. At 2am Delta CANCELED the flight and we all had to find hotels and didn't get a flight to late the next day. I didn't even have time to cancel one of my excursions with a 24 hour notice. Getting any sort of refund is a NIGHTMARE.

San Juan to Paris via New York JFK. The experience has been a very good one on the cabin service side, on time departures and luggage delivery. This has been expressed to Delta in most occasions. As a negative result of the Covid-19 pandemic, the service has suffered. On April 4th, the departure gate for the JFK to Paris changed and there was no App notification. After 5 hours wait at the original gate 32, we missed the flight. The Help Desk Agent informed us that our names were called many times for boarding, but at the new gate, where we were not at, obviously. It should have been done on the PA, to be heard everywhere. Delta booked us the next day on Air France, issued new boarding passes, luggage tags and informed that the four suitcases would be transferred to arrive on the flight with us. Upon arrival, a day later in Paris, the suitcases were missing. Two hours later the claim was processed through Air France and arrived 6 days later in terrible state, delivered by Chronopost. Fortunately, we traveled for a long stay. Tourists staying for only a week in Paris would have been very inconvenienced. The departure gate change was advised by email, not on the App. Supposedly the App does not have the interface. This seems hard to believe… being a very important issue. We encourage Delta to try harder.

My family and I flew Delta Air Lines where both of my luggage I checked in, I paid for. My 15 year old son who plays sports, is an A & B student, works Friday nights as a busboy, came to me asking where the gift box from Bath & Bodywork’s went being that he bought this as a gift for his girlfriend. Several of our items went missing (that could not be carried on) and when I filed a complaint with Delta, they stated they cannot do anything about it or investigate as this would be providing me preferential treatment. In the event you are traveling, do not check anything that might be attractive for one to steal. Delta had my luggage delivered within 12 hrs of the flight at 5:38AM where they left personal items on my front porch without a signature. They have to do better on protecting one’s personal items you pay to have delivered - at minimum, offer insurance if they’re going to allow for their associates to remove and steal items from your luggage. Super disappointed. A huge reminder on how not to treat customers…

We flew from Atlanta to Cape Town and Back after month. Both times flying we had our baby stroller and car seat checked in. Upon receiving our stroller, the wheel was cracked, as well as the seat was completely snapped. This is an incredibly expensive and well built stroller. It was mishandled in a very inappropriate way. We reached out to the airline a day late per their 7 day policy, we understood completely that we did that, the reasoning was simple, we were in a strange country with no signal and had to figure out what to do. Delta took about 3 days to tell us what to do, that gave us 3 days to handle it and acquire signal, with a baby adjusting to a new country. My wife has been a member of delta for over 6 years and swears by them, she only flies delta. Our claim was initiated and we were very polite to communicate. This was Deltas head baggage control managers response. He said that we aren't at all going to issue anything. He left us with a delta $100 travel voucher. He was rude, he never replied to the email that we sent back begging to help us and understand that we were a day late on our claim. We need a stroller, that's what our baby needs, Delta broke it and they aren't taking any responsibility.

Worst flight I have ever had. To begin with I checked a bag, $30 fee, then after i paid, he gave me the bag back and told me to lug it to the other side of the terminal. The plane had No tv, my tray was broken and horrible service! One way trip, $134 + baggage fee and the oldest airplane i have ever seen.  I was waiting for Austin Powers to come out. Absolutely horrible.

Worst first class experience ever! 1st problem was food. I've flown first class plenty of times and always have 3 choices for dinner. This flight attendant came by to get orders but stated they only have chicken Caesar salad, ugh. I do not eat that, coach had chicken Caesar salad, chicken salad sandwich and some sort of charcuterie option! Now granted they were going to pay extra for ordering any of those but I paid ahead by flying first class and had one nasty option. The flight attendant didn't like that I don't like Caesar salad and gave me attitude the rest of the flight. We also had no WiFi. Going into Hawaii every family needs agricultural forms to fill out, this flight didn't have enough forms and NO pens! They told us a million times the flight was full, so take your own advice and have enough stuff for everyone. They made everyone who did not have a form stay on the flight until they got more forms and filled them out. As a first class passenger with my bags tagged priority they are supposed to come out first, we'll they came out almost last! Flight home, no drinks while waiting for everyone to board, my husband had no blanket and pillow like everyone else in first class, he asked for it, they brought him two blankets because they were out of pillows. He ordered a special meal which they did NOT have!! The only option for dinner was a turkey sandwich and chips! These flight attendants do not call you by name, they could care less that I spent $2000 more per ticket than those sitting in coach and I might as well have been there! Nothing special at all about Delta first class! I will never fly them again, coach or first class

I booked a flight for unaccompanied minors through a delta agent on the phone (credit card rewards had to be done this way). The agent completely ruined our experience. It was not until I arrived at the airport for check in that I was told there was a $300 fee ($150 each way) for the unaccompanied minors. I have sent them on other airlines without this charge. In addition to that, the return flight was booked as the last flight out. It’s Delta policy that they cannot take the last flight in case something happened and the minors were stranded. Makes complete sense, but again we were not informed of this issue when the DELTA agent booked it for us. This caused us to reschedule to another day, waste trips and parking fees to the airport, and take another day off work. So a rundown of surprise expenses due to the inept booking agent: Minor fee- $300 Airport parking- $30 Gas and taking time off work- $$ The airport agents were extremely understanding but limited with what they could offer. After submitting a claim explaining all of this with receipts, customer service granted me a $25 voucher for future flights. Use another airline that actually cares if things go wrong, especially when responsible.

One of the best customer service I’ve ever seen, no delays, staff is very cheerful, in general 10/10. Trip took even less than should be, so I had additional time between flights. Wifi worked well too.

The agent at the desk was the rudest person I have ever met at any airport with any airline. I fly for business. The agent was completely uncaring and rude. I had a procedure on my foot the day prior to my 5:30am flight. Didn’t get a wheelchair which was (obviously) a mistake on my part. Because at 5:00am there was a huge line at TSA. I asked the TSA there about getting one, but that meant starting the process all over again by leaving the TSA line and going back up front. As it turned out, TSA opened another line and the process sped up. I hobbled to Gate 5 (at exactly 5:20) where the agent never looked up and said (and I quote) What’s your last name? & Gate 7. I hobbled over there and saw that wasn’t my flight, so I hobbled back  to Gate 5, where the agent was just wrapping up and getting ready to leave that area. I told him that’s not my flight and while I’m talking, he’s doing his work thing and moving to his next Gate. He has an accent so it was a little difficult to understand him, but what made it worse was that he now has his back to me and walking away. I have to say to him, “Dude, don’t make me chase after you in this”! Pointing to my boot. That’s when he told me, the flight took off already. It was 5:24 when he said this words and kept moving.  When I got to Gate 7 (new gate) the same agent was there (I guess he went the long way around or something, and recommended that I should have got a wheelchair! He was just a wry rude and inconsiderate human who should not be the first representative that Delta Clients have to speak with. Delta, you can have a better person as your initial contact.

Absolutely horrible! Worst flight we've ever had, and we've flown to 6 continents and over 20 countries on dozens or airlines. The cabin staff was rude and unaccommodating. The food was poor at best, and mostly inedible. They delayed/misplaced one of our check-in bags, the one that had our house keys in it. It was our fault to leave our keys in a checked in bag, trusting the bag would make it to our final destination. But it was almost 24 hours until our bag was returned. We spent the night in a hotel near our house until someone could let us in the next day. Delta is the only airline in my entire history of flying which has lost my luggage .. twice now. They also removed and discarded all of our personal luggage tags. We have no idea why they would do this. Also, on our initial Comfort Plus flight out of the country to London, they cancelled my ticket when I stated I was having a slight allergic reaction to something. This is understandable. But they would not let me fly again without a doctors note stating I could do so. The next day when we tried to book another flight, they charged us an additional $700 each and placed us in the back of Economy. No Comfort Plus seats, which we'd paid for. With the additional $700, this made our tickets $1700 each, one way. Do not fly this airline. It's not worth whatever price savings you may receive.

Customer service was bad. Rude and unhelpful. I had a fragile touchscreen with me in a padded bag as part of a conference display. I told the check in person it was fragile and they didn’t put fragile stickers on it. They forced me to check the fragile item in and when it arrived it was very badly broken. A crack right down the middle of the screen and other damage.

My wife and I flew First Class and Delta One flights from Cleveland, Ohio to Maui and back and had the following problems and delays on our flights. The first issue was on February 18, 2023 with our Delta One flight from Atlanta when we had to sit on the plane for over an hour because of some unknown maintenance issues before taking off late. Once we landed at Kahului, HI airport we sat on the tarmac for two hours before pulling up to the gate to unload. We were told by the pilot the reason was we had such a strong tailwind arriving early (not the case) along with many planes ahead of us that needed unloaded too. Thus, everyone had to sit on this plane for over 13 hours! On February 25, 2023 on our return Flight from Maui, HI we boarded the plane 1.5 hours later than scheduled because our plane had been sitting out on the tarmac for some reason waiting to pull up the the gate delaying our take-off by this long. When we boarded our Flight in Atlanta to return to Cleveland we sat in the plane for 1.5 hours being told by the pilot there were maintenance issues and ultimately had to add more fuel to the plane and waiting for the fuel truck to arrive with no estimated time being provided by anyone. All of these delays seem to be preventable by improvements in customer service i.e., better communications and your maintenance practices such as fuelling planes needs improved. These flights were a very disappointing experience. Then we are offered a $75 voucher eCredit, which is not useful. No interested in flying Delta after all these issues.

I wanted to give a special shout out to crew member (Tara) on flight DL 179 from Amsterdam to PDX on April 3 2023. She was extremely friendly, always smiling and so courteous in her language. Her manners are 10+. She kept everyone happy and made sure everybody had what they needed during whole flight! Please pass to her our words of appreciation!

1st Delta changed all 4 of our family's seats and double booked our 13 year old child and he was forced to stand for part of the flight. Then we landed in Atlanta and our connecting flight was cancelled to Huntsville, AL. They of course blamed weather so they wouldn't have to be held accountable although a flight (DL1726) flew out of Atlanta to Huntsville an hour later. We were told at the ticket counter our next available flight would be 3 days later and if that wasn't "good enough" we would have to choose ground transportation and we would be reimbursed for the flight not taken and a rental car. We were given the runaround for over 8 weeks now and over 5 hours of our time for Delta to offer us $25 credit towards our cancelled flight (not money back) and now they won't even reimburse us our $275 rental car to drive through the night to get our child to NASA Space camp on a Sunday. Very Disappointed!

Terrible experience! My flight is delayed more than 4 hours and got $15 complimentary voucher! Are you real Delta? What can you buy with $15? 2x drinks +2x chips was $34! Worst customer service and aggressive attitude! Will never fly Delta again!

We arrived in Detroit from Orlando and was expecting an approximate 1 hour lay over before heading to Buffalo International. Upon arrival in Detroit I received a notification that our flight would be delayed until 02:30. It was subsequently delayed two more times I believe, and then as we were preparing to board at nearly 03:00 the flight was canceled due to the pilot calling off or something to do with the pilot. Several Delta flights that night were delayed or canceled and I heard staffing mentioned by personnel for at two other gates. There were not other flights going out due to it being 3am and no other flights to Buffalo until approximately 36 hours later. I think there were only 3-4 Delta personnel in the airport to assist us but none of them could really help. I actually felt bad for them as they were taking a lot of grief from some very frustrated people. I connected with Delta at one of their service phones and was not really given any options and asked for a refund on my tickets so I could try to find alternate transport for my family of 6. I was told I would get $60 per ticket due to us completing the first leg of the trip (Orlando to Detroit). That was the morning of 7/22. It is 8/9/23 now and after a lot of follow up between Delta and the booking company, I am finally getting confirmation of the refund. However, I am only being refunded $32.14 per ticket, not $60. Since Delta could not fulfill their obligation to get my family home, we had to rent a vehicle, drive 6 hours home after spending 7 hours in the airport. I had to return that vehicle the following morning, to Detroit, so another 6 hours on the road. Since there are no one way rentals out of Detroit. I then had to wait approximately 5 hours in the airport to fly back to Buffalo. I was at least able to get my family home ... the new plane ticket cost me about $500 and the rental was around $250 and instead of getting home early Saturday morning, I spent the entire weekend traveling and did not get complete the trip until Sunday night. I am getting a refund of approximately $180 from Delta. I will never intentionally fly into Detroit airport and if I can help it, I will never fly Delta again. This has been a horrible experience overall.

Delta delayed our first flight causing us to miss our connection and we missed an entire day of our 6 day vacation in Paris. Delta refused to put us up when it stranded us in JFK overnight. They blamed the weather however it was notably clear in both our departure and destination cities and the gate agent couldn't tell us where the inclement weather was alleged to be. I suspect they blamed the weather to avoid compensating a plane full of disgruntled passengers who's travel plans were delayed.

I booked a Delta Comfort+ return flight Brussels-New York for 4 people. According to the Delta advertisement, a comfortable option with free (alcoholic) drinks, snacks and other gifts. On the outward flight in an old aircraft, two of the four video screens were broken. The food was bad, the chicken still raw. No snacks, no coffee or tea, and the drink was limited to 1 glass and a bottle of water. On the way back a totally unintelligible call system, the same drink restriction, no snacks and a breakfast that even my dog ​​would refuse. Personnel? It clearly worked against his will.

Delta should have never booked this flight! Several of us missed our connecting flight in Seattle to Tokyo. We were coming from Portland. Even if they would have left on time, I don't think we would have made our flight. This resulted in my missing an entire day of my vacation in Tokyo.

We had horrendous flights especially on the way to Jackson Hole and delays returning BOTH due to maintenance issues. On the way we had to overnight in St. Paul because we missed our connection, then took at 7am flight to Salt lake where we paid $770 to rent a car and drive 5 hours to get to Jackson. After multiple calls to CS and turn downs, I sent a FEDEX to the CEO requesting reimbursement for the additional car cost with receipts, nothing more, they didn't pay us the courtesy to respond. What kind of a CEO and airlines disses their clients like that - it's so disturbing.

I was big fan of Delta. Looks Delta has loses it credibility of in time flights. In the past 2 months I flew 6 to 8 times. In this route through Delta 80% of time the flight was delayed. Every time standard answer - saying maintenance issues, engineers are looking into it. I think they should change their name from Delta to Delayta. Thinking to change the airlines

I had two flights from MSP to SFO scheduled, one at 2:20 PM and one at 6:55 PM due to weather delays which moved my flight to a day later. I understand the part where my flight got delayed due to weather. But I reached out to the support chat for getting my boarding pass which I couldn’t get online for some reason. I couldn’t call then since it was a long wait on call support. They responded after 3 hrs, which is again fine by me, i asked them to get me a boarding pass for 2:20 pm flight. They told me it was cancelled. I asked them multiple times if they meant the flight was cancelled or the ticket got canceled they kept saying the flight was cancelled. Now I had to accept obviously even though I couldn’t see anything on the internet about that. They gave me the boarding pass for 6:55 pm flight though and quickly removed my previous flight from my profile which i could see before the chat. I now saw that the 2:20 pm flight has departed and it was not cancelled at all. I have the evidence that i was booked on that flight in screenshots, but i never got the ticket which is what i was asking the support for. I was also assigned a seat. Even though they put me in first class seat for my 6:55 flight, i wanted to reach my destination earlier. And it’s not fair to lie to your customers, when they really trust you and reach out for support.

After my flight from SD to SLC was delayed, I missed my flight to Dallas. I walked towards Delta customer service near A15 when in order to find the quickest flight to Dallas. One of the customer service agents found a direct flight with the earliest arrival at 9:19PM. I asked the agent whether they can upgrade my seat for the inconvenience since I payed to insure my ticket. The gentleman sent to a lady and she said she “I upgraded you to Comfort+”. That made me feel good until I scanned my ticket to board and they did not upgrade my seat.

We had a layover in SLC on our trip from Denver to Honolulu. The SLC flight was repeatedly delayed until it ended up being delayed OVER 12 HOURS! Due maintenance and pilot issues. What is absolutely UNACCEPTABLE is that as compensation for a lost day of vacation, lost day of rental car and lost night of hotel we were offered 8000 Sky miles points for two of us (who are NOT sky miles members). This is the equivalent to approximately $89. Our third person is being offered a $65 credit to be used on a future Delta flight. This is the most ridiculous amount of compensation I can even imagine. POOR POOR customer service for a MAJOR flight issue that was completely their fault.

The flight was delayed 3 times and the gate changed to across the airport. On the flight, the air conditioning was blowing warm air, at least that's what the cockpit said. Nobody in the cabin felt it. Anyway, instead of carrying on another 25 minutes to our scheduled stop, we diverted to Nashville, which took us 20 minutes in the opposite directing to get to. Because safety. When we arrived, we were directed to a gate across the airport just to get any information. But they didn't have any! Eventually they got us another flight scheduled for 3 hours after we landed, but then even that one was delayed several times. I missed my connector with another airline and Delta's answer was "not our problem. We got you to your destination." Luckily United was understanding, but I was still delayed over 24 hours. Actually as I'm writing this, I'm still stuck in Nashville. Anyway, the weather today was actually quite nice, so the only reason I can think of for this many delays is straight up incompetence, originating in Atlanta and reverberating through their counters in Nashville. This is the first Delta flight I've been on in years, and I hope it's the last!

I do not recommend this route. When I purchased the ticket, Delta was partnered with Air France. I fly this route often and when operated by Air France, it was a much better experience. Delta’s LAX to Paris now uses a domestic terminal with no access to duty free or the good airport lounges. We had to use the Delta One Lounge which was the McDonalds of airport lounges. When we boarded the plane they did not offer champagne. They had Sparkling wine.  The business class cabin was mediocre and the the goody bag was kitch environmental rubbish. I enjoy flying International because usually it is luxurious and a highlight of the trip. This was a little better than a long domestic flight which is sad. Won’t make this mistake again! Don’t fly Delta International out of LAX. Delta, stay in your lane and keep to domestic flights. Sub out your international flights to companies that remember what service and class was.

Original departure time was 10:05am. when I arrived 2 hours early, the plane had too much fuel on board, so after an hour or so they had to send a truck to empty some fuel. the truck they sent was full, so had to wait another hour or so for a new truck to come get some fuel out. We had boarded the plane, by that time, a crew member timed out. We were de-boarded because "Delta cant have you on the plane for 3 hours (probably because they'd have to refund us at that time) and we were told "there is another crew member on standby at the lounge. They should be here in about 10 minutes" - an hour later, still no crew member. By the time the replacement crew member got to us, a pilot had to be replaced due to their time being up as well. Another hour or so later, another plane pulled up to the next gate and we inherited their crew and pilots. Finally boarded again, and after 30 min on the tarmack, were told the plane needed fuel so we were waiting for a fuel truck. An hour later we were waiting on a fuel slip after the truck was done and we were good to depart. Absolutely ridiculous experience. I paid almost $1000 for this trip and specifically chose delta because this experience isn't typical to them. In hindsight, i should have flown another airline. I probably would have had a similar experience with Spirit, but it would have only cost me half of what delta did.  One more addition. My return flight was delayed due to "mechanical issue" which was later described in an email from delta as "damage to the aircraft" - on that flight customers were offered $800 to switch to a later flight. I paid $1000, for that trip, and delta refused to refund any more than $140. After my email response, i was told any more communication about this issue would be pointless.

I had the nightmare flight experience from Delta Airlines. My recent trip from Boston to Shanghai was a complete disaster. Delta Airline flight DL509 got delayed 3 hours and made me arrive late in Los Angeles from Boston. This made me miss my connecting flight to Shanghai with China Airlines. Second, Delta Airlines changed my entire trip without notifying me. I found out my flight changed the next day from my travel agent. Delta changed my entire trip and made me use their airline to fly back to China instead of flying with China Airlines. The new trip now forced me to fly to Seattle and then fly to China through Delta Airlines. This forced me to stay in a hotel for 2 nights and the 2nd night hotel I had to pay out of pocket. Third, Delta Airlines then tried to make me purchase a Main Cabin class ticket for the 3rd day flight to China on their Airlines. This was an $800 ticket which they said I have to purchase in in order to get on the only next available flight to China. So, for them making me miss my connecting flight I had to pay $800 just to fly back and also I had to spend $200 the second night for a hotel in Seattle so I can catch the flight to China the 3rd day is ridiculous. Finally, when I took their flight to China on the third day and arrived in China they lost my luggage. I had important items in my luggage which I had to give to my relatives and now its gone and they are still trying to find it. Delta mentioned specifically that I did not need to worry about my luggage when my flight got changed by them. This entire trip made me feel like a helpless homeless person for 3 days and as an old 70 year old man my health deteriorated greatly from the stress they caused me. There’s no shame in the horrendous customer service they provided and I was never compensated by Delta Airlines for the distress they caused me.
